31.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, round whose neck is swinging a garland of flowers beautified with the moon-locket, whose two hands are adorned with the flute and jeweled ornaments, who always revels in pastimes of love, whose graceful threefold-bending form of Śyāmasundara is eternally manifest.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific item is noted as beautifying the garland that swings around the neck of Lord Govinda?
options: The Kaustubha jewel
A moon-locket
A peacock feather
The Srivatsa mark
questions.correct_answer: A moon-locket
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions Govinda wears a garland of flowers that is beautified with a moon-locket.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific nature of the graceful form of Śyāmasundara that is eternally manifest?
options: A four-armed form
A dancing posture
A threefold-bending form
A reclining form
questions.correct_answer: A threefold-bending form
questions.explanation: The text describes Govinda as having a graceful threefold-bending form of Śyāmasundara which is eternally manifest.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which two primary items adorn the two hands of the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: A lotus flower and a conch shell
A flute and jeweled ornaments
A discus and a mace
A butter ball and a flute
questions.correct_answer: A flute and jeweled ornaments
questions.explanation: The verse states that Govinda's two hands are adorned with the flute and jeweled ornaments.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is Govinda, the primeval Lord, described as always performing?
options: Creating the material universes
Reveling in pastimes of love
Protecting the demigods from demons
Calculating the duration of time
questions.correct_answer: Reveling in pastimes of love
questions.explanation: The text describes Govinda as the primeval Lord who always revels in pastimes of love.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the unique characteristic of the garland worn by the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: It is made of forest flowers and beautified with a moon-locket
It is made of golden lotuses and scented with sandalwood
It is made of peacock feathers and divine gems
It is made of tulasi leaves and fragrant Parijata flowers
questions.correct_answer: It is made of forest flowers and beautified with a moon-locket
questions.explanation: The verse describes Govinda wearing a garland of flowers that is beautified specifically with a moon-locket.

32.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, whose transcendental form is full of bliss, truth, substantiality and is thus full of the most dazzling splendor. Each of the limbs of that transcendental figure possesses in Himself, the full-fledged functions of all the organs, and eternally sees, maintains and manifests the infinite universes, both spiritual and mundane.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what unique transcendental capability does each limb of Govinda’s divine form possess?
options: Each limb can manifest multiple avatars simultaneously in various planetary systems.
Each limb possesses the full-fledged functions of all the other bodily organs.
Each limb radiates a different color of the spiritual effulgence.
Each limb can independently grant liberation to the conditioned souls.
questions.correct_answer: Each limb possesses the full-fledged functions of all the other bodily organs.
questions.explanation: The text states that each of the limbs of that transcendental figure possesses in Himself, the full-fledged functions of all the organs.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the transcendental form of Govinda?
options: Full of bliss, truth, substantiality, and dazzling splendor.
Formless, eternal, all-pervading, and beyond material definition.
Represented by the golden effulgence of the Brahman light.
A manifestation of the three modes of material nature in perfection.
questions.correct_answer: Full of bliss, truth, substantiality, and dazzling splendor.
questions.explanation: The verse describes Govinda as having a transcendental form that is full of bliss, truth, substantiality, and is thus full of the most dazzling splendor.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the eternal role of Govinda’s limbs in relation to the cosmic creation?
options: To engage in the pastimes of killing demons in the mundane realm.
To divide the material energy into the three modes of goodness, passion, and ignorance.
To eternally see, maintain, and manifest the infinite spiritual and mundane universes.
To serve as the resting place for the souls seeking refuge in the Causal Ocean.
questions.correct_answer: To eternally see, maintain, and manifest the infinite spiritual and mundane universes.
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Govinda’s transcendental figure eternally sees, maintains and manifests the infinite universes, both spiritual and mundane.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what geometric shape serves as the abode for the predominated and predominating aspects of the Absolute?
options: A circular lotus with a thousand petals.
A triangular structure representing the three energies.
A hexagonal figure with sixfold divisions.
A square altar containing the divine mantra.
questions.correct_answer: A hexagonal figure with sixfold divisions.
questions.explanation: The scripture describes the whorl of the transcendental lotus where Kṛṣṇa dwells as a hexagonal figure, which is also the abode of the predominated and predominating aspect.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the holy name that is manifested within the hexagonal figure of the spiritual abode?
options: A secret mantra consisting of twelve transcendental syllables.
A powerful hymn composed of one hundred and eight sacred names.
The holy name consisting of eighteen transcendental letters.
The primordial Omkara sound vibration from which all life springs.
questions.correct_answer: The holy name consisting of eighteen transcendental letters.
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the holy name consisting of eighteen transcendental letters is manifested in a hexagonal figure with sixfold divisions.

1.verse_text: Kṛṣṇa who is known as Govinda is the Supreme Godhead. He has an eternal blissful spiritual body. He is the origin of all. He has no other origin and He is the prime cause of all causes.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the body of Kṛṣṇa, who is known as Govinda?
options: A material body composed of the five elements
An eternal blissful spiritual body
A temporary form assumed for cosmic creation
An impersonal effulgence devoid of limbs
questions.correct_answer: An eternal blissful spiritual body
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa, known as Govinda, possesses an eternal blissful spiritual body.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is Kṛṣṇa's position regarding the cause of all causes?
options: He is one of many causes among the demigods
He is the secondary cause of the material manifestation
He is the prime cause of all causes
He is the result of the interaction of prakṛti and puruṣa
questions.correct_answer: He is the prime cause of all causes
questions.explanation: The scripture defines Govinda as the origin of all and the prime cause of all causes.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what can be said about the origin of Kṛṣṇa?
options: He has many origins from previous manifestations
He originated from the causal ocean
He has no other origin
He originated from the light of the impersonal Brahman
questions.correct_answer: He has no other origin
questions.explanation: It is stated that Kṛṣṇa is the origin of all and He Himself has no other origin.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the spiritual abode of Gokula described in relation to the lotus?
options: It is a vast field of white light
It is a whorl of leaves with thousands of petals
It is a golden city situated in the causal ocean
It is an island made of eight jewels
questions.correct_answer: It is a whorl of leaves with thousands of petals
questions.explanation: The text describes Gokula as a superexcellent station having thousands of petals and a corolla like a lotus.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what part of the lotus serves as the actual abode of Kṛṣṇa within Gokula?
options: The outermost petals
The stalk of the lotus
The whorl of the leaves
The stem beneath the water
questions.correct_answer: The whorl of the leaves
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the whorl of the leaves is the actual abode of Kṛṣṇa.

2.verse_text: [The spiritual place of transcendental pastimes of Kṛṣṇa is portrayed in the second verse.] The superexcellent station of Kṛṣṇa, which is known as Gokula, has thousands of petals and a corolla like that of a lotus sprouted from a part of His infinitary aspect, the whorl of the leaves being the actual abode of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific name of the superexcellent transcendental station where Kṛṣṇa enjoys His eternal pastimes?
options: Vaikuṇṭha
Gokula
Mathurā
Dvārakā
questions.correct_answer: Gokula
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names the superexcellent station of Kṛṣṇa as Gokula.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, from what specific aspect of the Lord does the lotus of Gokula manifest its thousands of petals and corolla?
options: His infinitary aspect
His Vaikuṇṭha expansion
His form of Mahā-Viṣṇu
His Nārāyaṇa manifestation
questions.correct_answer: His infinitary aspect
questions.explanation: The text states that the lotus of Gokula is sprouted from a part of His infinitary aspect.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what part of the transcendental lotus specifically serves as the actual abode for Kṛṣṇa?
options: The outermost petals
The golden corolla
The whorl of the leaves
The stem of the lotus
questions.correct_answer: The whorl of the leaves
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the whorl of the leaves is the actual abode of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, the structure of Gokula possesses a corolla that is compared to what type of botanical flower?
options: A jasmine flower
A rose flower
A lotus flower
A kadamba flower
questions.correct_answer: A lotus flower
questions.explanation: The text compares the corolla of Gokula's transcendental station to that of a lotus.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the text describe the quantity of petals associated with the superexcellent station of Gokula?
options: One hundred petals
One thousand petals
Ten thousand petals
Innumerable petals
questions.correct_answer: One thousand petals
questions.explanation: The text describes the station of Gokula as having thousands of petals.

3.verse_text: The whorl of that transcendental lotus is the realm wherein dwells Kṛṣṇa. It is a hexagonal figure, the abode of the indwelling predominated and predominating aspect of the Absolute. Like a diamond the central supporting figure of self-luminous Kṛṣṇa stands as the transcendental source of all potencies. The holy name consisting of eighteen transcendental letters is manifested in a hexagonal figure with sixfold divisions.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific geometric shape of the whorl of the transcendental lotus in Gokula where Kṛṣṇa resides?
options: A circular figure with twelve divisions
A hexagonal figure with sixfold divisions
A square figure with fourfold divisions
A triangular figure with threefold divisions
questions.correct_answer: A hexagonal figure with sixfold divisions
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes the whorl of the transcendental lotus as a hexagonal figure containing sixfold divisions, which also manifests the eighteen-syllable holy name.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the central supporting figure found within the hexagonal whorl of the transcendental lotus?
options: The expansion of Sankarṣaṇa in a meditative pose
The effulgent form of Śrī Rādhikā surrounded by sakhīs
The self-luminous form of Kṛṣṇa standing like a diamond
The majestic throne of Maha-Viṣṇu radiating pure light
questions.correct_answer: The self-luminous form of Kṛṣṇa standing like a diamond
questions.explanation: The text describes the central supporting figure of the self-luminous Kṛṣṇa standing like a diamond, serving as the source of all potencies.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the identity of the hexagonal whorl of the transcendental lotus in relation to the Absolute Truth?
options: It is the realm of the indwelling predominated and predominating aspect of the Absolute
It is the entrance to the Vaikuṇṭha planets where the four-armed forms reside
It is the garden of the expansion of the internal potency of the Lord
It is the seat of the impersonal Brahman effulgence
questions.correct_answer: It is the realm of the indwelling predominated and predominating aspect of the Absolute
questions.explanation: The verse clearly states that this hexagonal realm is the abode of both the predominated and predominating aspects of the Absolute.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what holy mantra is manifested within the hexagonal divisions of the transcendental whorl?
options: The ten-syllable mantra of Gopāla
The eighteen-syllable mantra
The twelve-syllable mantra of Vasudeva
The twenty-four-syllable Gāyatrī mantra
questions.correct_answer: The eighteen-syllable mantra
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the holy name consisting of eighteen transcendental letters is manifested within the hexagonal figure of the whorl.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the role of the central figure of Kṛṣṇa standing in the hexagonal whorl?
options: He acts as the guardian of the eight directions of the material world
He is the transcendental source of all potencies
He is the one who manifests the various incarnations for the creation
He is the judge who assigns the fruits of work to all souls
questions.correct_answer: He is the transcendental source of all potencies
questions.explanation: The text describes the central figure of Kṛṣṇa as standing like a diamond and explicitly identifies Him as the transcendental source of all potencies.

4.verse_text: The whorl of that eternal realm Gokula is the hexagonal abode of Kṛṣṇa. Its petals are the abodes of gopīs who are part and parcel of Kṛṣṇa to whom they are most lovingly devoted and are similar in essence. The petals shine beautifully like so many walls. The extended leaves of that lotus are the gardenlike dhāma, i.e. spiritual abode of Śrī Rādhikā, the most beloved of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what geometric shape forms the central, eternal abode of Lord Kṛṣṇa within the realm of Gokula?
options: A circular disc
A hexagonal whorl
A square plateau
A thousand-petaled lotus
questions.correct_answer: A hexagonal whorl
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes the whorl of the eternal realm Gokula as the hexagonal abode of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who dwells upon the petals that surround the hexagonal central abode of Kṛṣṇa?
options: The four-armed forms of Vāsudeva
The gopīs who are part and parcel of Kṛṣṇa
The incarnations of the ten dik-pālas
The sages who represent the four Vedas
questions.correct_answer: The gopīs who are part and parcel of Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The text states that the petals of the lotus are the abodes of the gopīs who are lovingly devoted to Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific area within the transcendental lotus represents the garden-like spiritual abode of Śrī Rādhikā?
options: The central diamond-like supporting figure
The hexagonal whorl
The extended leaves of the lotus
The walls surrounding the petals
questions.correct_answer: The extended leaves of the lotus
questions.explanation: The text identifies the extended leaves of that lotus as the dhāma of Śrī Rādhikā, the most beloved of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, to whom are the gopīs inhabiting the lotus petals compared in terms of their essence?
options: They are similar in essence to Kṛṣṇa
They are expansions of the internal potency
They are manifestations of the four Vedas
They are reflections of the goddess of fortune
questions.correct_answer: They are similar in essence to Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The scripture notes that the gopīs, being part and parcel of Kṛṣṇa, are similar in essence to Him.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the appearance of the petal-walls surrounding the abode of Kṛṣṇa described?
options: They shine beautifully like walls
They are made of the eight jewels
They are guarded by ten tridents
They glow like molten gold
questions.correct_answer: They shine beautifully like walls
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the petals of the hexagonal abode shine beautifully like so many walls.

5.verse_text: [The surrounding external plane of Gokula is described in this verse.] There is a mysterious quadrangular place named Śvetadvīpa surrounding the outskirts of Gokula. Śvetadvīpa is divided into four parts on all sides. The abode of Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna and Aniruddha are separately located in each of these four parts. These four divided abodes are enveloped by the fourfold human requirements such as piety, wealth, passion and liberation, as also by the four Vedas, viz., Ṛg, Sāma, Yajur and Atharva, which deal with the mantra and which are the bases of achievements of the fourfold mundane requirements. Ten tridents are fixed in the ten directions, including the zenith and nadir. The eight directions are decorated with the eight jewels of Mahāpadma, Padma, Śaṅkha, Makara, Kacchapa, Mukunda, Kunda, and Nīla. There are ten protectors [dik-pālas] of the ten directions in the form of mantra. The associates of the hues of blue, yellow, red and white and the extraordinary potencies bearing the names of Vimala, etc., shine on all sides.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the mysterious, quadrangular abode that surrounds the outskirts of Gokula?
options: Svetadvipa
Anantadvipa
Vaikunthadvipa
Yogapitha
questions.correct_answer: Svetadvipa
questions.explanation: The text describes Svetadvipa as a mysterious quadrangular place located in the outskirts surrounding Gokula.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which four divine expansions of the Personality of Godhead reside in the separately divided parts of Svetadvipa?
options: Vasudeva, Sankarshana, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha
Narayana, Vishnu, Padmanabha, and Trivikrama
Rama, Krishna, Balarama, and Narasimha
Vamana, Varaha, Matsya, and Kurma
questions.correct_answer: Vasudeva, Sankarshana, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the abodes of Vasudeva, Sankarshana, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha are separately located in the four parts of Svetadvipa.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what are the four Vedas that envelop the divided abodes within Svetadvipa, serving as the basis for mundane achievements?
options: Rg, Sama, Yajur, and Atharva
Rg, Sama, Yajur, and Itihasa
Rg, Sama, Atharva, and Purana
Yajur, Atharva, Upanishad, and Vedanta
questions.correct_answer: Rg, Sama, Yajur, and Atharva
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the four Vedas—Rg, Sama, Yajur, and Atharva—which deal with the mantra, envelop the abodes within Svetadvipa.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how many jewels are specifically mentioned as decorating the eight directions of Svetadvipa?
options: Eight
Ten
Four
Twelve
questions.correct_answer: Eight
questions.explanation: The text lists eight specific jewels—Mahapadma, Padma, Sankha, Makara, Kacchapa, Mukunda, Kunda, and Nila—that decorate the eight directions.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how many tridents are described as being fixed in the ten directions, including the zenith and nadir, of the Svetadvipa region?
options: Ten
Eight
Four
Sixteen
questions.correct_answer: Ten
questions.explanation: The text specifies that ten tridents are fixed in the ten directions, which encompass the eight cardinal points plus the zenith and nadir.

6.verse_text: The Lord of Gokula is the transcendental Supreme Godhead, the own Self of eternal ecstasies. He is the superior of all superiors and is busily engaged in the enjoyments of the transcendental realm and has no association with His mundane potency.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the fundamental relationship between the Lord of Gokula and the mundane potency of this material world?
options: He is constantly interacting with her to maintain the cosmic order
He is busily engaged in the enjoyments of the transcendental realm and has no association with her
He directly controls her through his expansion as Maha-Visnu
He maintains her as a necessary component of his internal pleasure potency
questions.correct_answer: He is busily engaged in the enjoyments of the transcendental realm and has no association with her
questions.explanation: The text states that the Lord of Gokula has no association with His mundane potency, as He is fully occupied with transcendental enjoyments.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the Lord of Gokula described in relation to all other entities and manifestations?
options: He is the first among equals in the spiritual sky
He is the servant of all His devotees
He is the superior of all superiors
He is the origin of the four-armed expansions only
questions.correct_answer: He is the superior of all superiors
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies the Lord of Gokula as the superior of all superiors, establishing His ultimate position.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what constitutes the very essence of the Lord of Gokula?
options: The collection of all material elements and energies
The own Self of eternal ecstasies
The aggregate of all demigods and their powers
The impersonal radiance of the Brahman effulgence
questions.correct_answer: The own Self of eternal ecstasies
questions.explanation: The verse defines the Lord of Gokula as the transcendental Supreme Godhead and the own Self of eternal ecstasies.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific title is given to the Lord who resides in Gokula?
options: The Supreme Godhead
The Master of the Causal Ocean
The Lord of the Material Universe
The Protector of the Ten Directions
questions.correct_answer: The Supreme Godhead
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies the Lord of Gokula as the transcendental Supreme Godhead.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what primary activity occupies the Lord of Gokula, distinguishing His existence from material concerns?
options: Overseeing the creation and dissolution of the material universes
Directly managing the affairs of the mundane potency
Engaging in the enjoyments of the transcendental realm
Meditating upon the welfare of the living entities in the material world
questions.correct_answer: Engaging in the enjoyments of the transcendental realm
questions.explanation: The text highlights that the Lord is busily engaged in the enjoyments of the transcendental realm, which is the reason He has no association with mundane potency.

7.verse_text: Kṛṣṇa never consorts with His illusory energy. Still her connection is not entirely cut off from the Absolute Truth. When He intends to create the material world the amorous pastime, in which He engages by consorting with His own spiritual [cit] potency Ramā by casting His glance at the deluding energy in the shape of sending His time energy, is an auxiliary activity.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific nature of the interaction between Kṛṣṇa and the illusory energy during the act of material creation?
options: He directly consorts with her
He does not consort with her
He merges into her
He is controlled by her
questions.correct_answer: He does not consort with her
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa never consorts with His illusory energy, despite her connection to the Absolute Truth not being entirely cut off.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does Kṛṣṇa initiate the creation of the material world without engaging directly with the deluding energy?
options: By expanding into Brahmā
By casting His glance at the deluding energy
By physically touching the material elements
By meditating upon the void
questions.correct_answer: By casting His glance at the deluding energy
questions.explanation: The text explains that the creation occurs when Kṛṣṇa casts His glance at the deluding energy by sending His time energy as an auxiliary activity.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the name of the spiritual potency with whom Kṛṣṇa engages in amorous pastimes while orchestrating creation?
options: Durgā
Rāmā
Yoganidrā
Māyā
questions.correct_answer: Rāmā
questions.explanation: The text identifies Rāmā as the spiritual [cit] potency with whom the Lord consorts during the time He intends to create the material world.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the glance that Kṛṣṇa casts toward the deluding energy during the creation process?
options: An auxiliary activity
The primary motive
A sign of His attachment
A destructive force
questions.correct_answer: An auxiliary activity
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that His consorting with His own spiritual potency while casting His glance at the deluding energy is an auxiliary activity.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is sent forth by Kṛṣṇa in the shape of the deluding energy to facilitate the creation of the mundane world?
options: His time energy
His internal potency
His plenary portion
His breath
questions.correct_answer: His time energy
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the glance at the deluding energy is performed in the shape of sending His time energy as an auxiliary activity.

8.verse_text: [The secondary process of association with Māyā is described.] Ramādevī, the spiritual [cit] potency, beloved consort of the Supreme Lord, is the regulatrix of all entities. The divine plenary portion of Kṛṣṇa creates the mundane world. At creation there appears a divine halo of the nature of His own subjective portion [svāṁśa]. This halo is divine Śambhu, the masculine symbol or manifested emblem of the Supreme Lord. This halo is the dim twilight reflection of the supreme eternal effulgence. This masculine symbol is the subjective portion of divinity who functions as progenitor of the mundane world, subject to the supreme regulatrix [niyati]. The conceiving potency in regard to mundane creation makes her appearance out of the supreme regulatrix. She is Māyā, the limited, nonabsolute [aparā] potency, the symbol of mundane feminine productivity. The intercourse of these two brings forth the faculty of perverted cognition, the reflection of the seed of the procreative desire of the Supreme Lord.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the divine halo that appears at the time of creation to serve as the masculine symbol of the Supreme Lord?
options: It is the direct form of Maha-Visnu
It is divine Śambhu
It is the manifestation of Ananta Sesa
It is the expansion of Lord Brahma
questions.correct_answer: It is divine Śambhu
questions.explanation: The text states that the divine halo appearing at creation, which serves as the masculine symbol or manifested emblem of the Supreme Lord, is divine Śambhu.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the nature of the divine halo of Śambhu described in relation to the supreme eternal effulgence?
options: As the direct and full brilliance
As the dim twilight reflection
As a shadow cast by the sun
As a mirror image of the internal potency
questions.correct_answer: As the dim twilight reflection
questions.explanation: The scripture describes the divine halo of Śambhu as being the dim twilight reflection of the supreme eternal effulgence of the Lord.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the role of Māyā in the process of mundane creation?
options: She acts as the supreme regulatrix of the spiritual realm
She is the symbol of mundane feminine productivity
She is the original creator of the spiritual effulgence
She is the expansion of Ramadevi herself
questions.correct_answer: She is the symbol of mundane feminine productivity
questions.explanation: The text identifies Māyā as the limited, nonabsolute potency who appears as the symbol of mundane feminine productivity.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what results from the intercourse between the masculine symbol Śambhu and the feminine productivity of Māyā?
options: The manifestation of the Cintamani-dhama
The appearance of the spiritual cit-potency
The faculty of perverted cognition
The eternal liberation of all conditioned souls
questions.correct_answer: The faculty of perverted cognition
questions.explanation: The text explains that the intercourse of the masculine symbol and the feminine productive potency brings forth the faculty of perverted cognition, which is a reflection of the Lord's procreative desire.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who is identified as the supreme regulatrix and the spiritual potency who serves as the beloved consort of the Supreme Lord?
options: Ramādevī
Durga
Yoganidrā
Sarasvatī
questions.correct_answer: Ramādevī
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names Ramādevī as the spiritual, cit potency and the regulatrix of all entities, serving as the beloved consort of the Lord.

9.verse_text: All offspring of the consort of the great lord [Maheśvara] of this mundane world are of the nature of the embodiment of the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the inherent nature of all offspring produced by the consort of the great lord, Maheśvara, within this mundane world?
options: They are embodiments of the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs
They are expansions of the spiritual potency of the Supreme Lord
They are emanations of the golden effulgence of Maha-Visnu
They are reflections of the eight jewels found in Svetadvipa
questions.correct_answer: They are embodiments of the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that all offspring of Maheśvara's consort are of the nature of the embodiment of the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the text characterize the offspring that arise from the interaction of the material causal principles?
options: As manifestations of pure spiritual bliss
As embodiments of mundane generative organs
As manifestations of the Lord's internal potency
As representations of the presiding demigods
questions.correct_answer: As embodiments of mundane generative organs
questions.explanation: The text confirms that within this mundane world, the offspring of the consort of the great lord are embodiment of the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary ontological status of the offspring generated through the consort of Maheśvara in the material realm?
options: They are direct expansions of the Cintamani-dhama
They are of the nature of mundane masculine and feminine generative organs
They are eternal associates of the Supreme Lord
They are manifestations of the shadow of the cit potency
questions.correct_answer: They are of the nature of mundane masculine and feminine generative organs
questions.explanation: The scripture defines the nature of these offspring specifically as the embodiment of mundane masculine and feminine generative organs.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific duality characterizes the offspring of the great lord of the mundane world?
options: The duality of spiritual and material energies
The duality of masculine and feminine generative organs
The duality of time and space
The duality of individual souls and the Supersoul
questions.correct_answer: The duality of masculine and feminine generative organs
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the offspring are embody the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs as their defining nature.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what do the offspring of the consort of the great lord (Maheśvara) represent within the context of mundane procreation?
options: The embodiment of mundane masculine and feminine generative organs
The expansion of the ten tridents of the dik-palas
The result of the glance of the Lord upon Maya
The manifestation of the sixteen kalas of the moon
questions.correct_answer: The embodiment of mundane masculine and feminine generative organs
questions.explanation: The text states that the nature of all such offspring is the embodiment of the mundane masculine and feminine generative organs.

10.verse_text: The person embodying the material causal principle, viz., the great lord of this mundane world [Maheśvara] Śambhu, in the form of the male generating organ, is joined to his female consort the limited energy [Māyā] as the efficient causal principle. The Lord of the world Mahā-Viṣṇu is manifest in him by His subjective portion in the form of His glance.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the manifestation of the great lord of this mundane world, Śambhu, described in relation to the causal principle?
options: As the embodiment of the male generating organ
As the eternal shadow of the spiritual effulgence
As the presiding deity of the Causal Ocean
As the guardian of the eight-petaled lotus
questions.correct_answer: As the embodiment of the male generating organ
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines Śambhu, the great lord of this mundane world, as the person embodying the material causal principle in the form of the male generating organ.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who acts as the female consort joined to Śambhu to function as the efficient causal principle?
options: Durga Devi
The limited energy known as Māyā
The cit potency
Yoganidrā
questions.correct_answer: The limited energy known as Māyā
questions.explanation: The verse states that Śambhu is joined to his female consort, who is identified as the limited energy, or Māyā, to serve as the efficient causal principle.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the Lord of the world, Mahā-Viṣṇu, manifest Himself within the form of Śambhu?
options: By His direct appearance in the form of a golden flame
By His subjective portion in the form of His glance
By the expansion of His internal potency
By breathing out the lords of the mundane worlds
questions.correct_answer: By His subjective portion in the form of His glance
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Mahā-Viṣṇu manifests within Śambhu by His subjective portion, which takes the form of His glance.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what role does the male generating organ of Śambhu play within the structure of this mundane world?
options: It acts as the efficient causal principle
It creates the spiritual abodes of the Vaikuntha planets
It maintains the balance of the Causal Ocean
It serves as the foundation for the eight jewels
questions.correct_answer: It acts as the efficient causal principle
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that Śambhu, in the form of the male generating organ, is joined to Māyā to operate as the efficient causal principle for the mundane world.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the connection between the limited energy (Māyā) and the great lord Śambhu?
options: She is his daughter
She is his female consort
She is his servant in the spiritual realm
She is the source of his divine knowledge
questions.correct_answer: She is his female consort
questions.explanation: The text explicitly refers to the limited energy, Māyā, as the female consort of Śambhu, the great lord of the mundane world.

11.verse_text: The Lord of the mundane world, Mahā-Viṣṇu, possesses thousands of thousands of heads, eyes, hands. He is the source of thousands of thousands of avatāras in His thousands of thousands of subjective portions. He is the creator of thousands of thousands of individual souls.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the text describe the physical manifestation of Mahā-Viṣṇu in terms of His limbs and sensory organs?
options: He possesses thousands of thousands of heads, eyes, and hands.
He is adorned with ten arms and infinite faces for every direction.
He has two arms holding a flute and an expansive crown.
He possesses four arms holding the conch, disc, club, and lotus.
questions.correct_answer: He possesses thousands of thousands of heads, eyes, and hands.
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the Lord of the mundane world, Mahā-Viṣṇu, possesses thousands of thousands of heads, eyes, and hands.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the role of Mahā-Viṣṇu in relation to the multitude of avatāras?
options: He is the source of thousands of thousands of avatāras.
He serves as the resting place for all avatāras after their pastimes.
He is the one who initiates the descent of avatāras into the spiritual sky.
He acts as the witness to the pastimes of the various avatāras.
questions.correct_answer: He is the source of thousands of thousands of avatāras.
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Mahā-Viṣṇu as the source of thousands of thousands of avatāras through His thousands of thousands of subjective portions.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is Mahā-Viṣṇu described as being in relation to individual souls?
options: He is the creator of thousands of thousands of individual souls.
He is the judge who assigns karma to individual souls.
He is the final destination for all individual souls upon liberation.
He is the protector of the individual souls in the material realm.
questions.correct_answer: He is the creator of thousands of thousands of individual souls.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Mahā-Viṣṇu as the creator of thousands of thousands of individual souls.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, through what manifestation does Mahā-Viṣṇu bring forth the multitude of avatāras?
options: His thousands of thousands of subjective portions.
His transcendental glance upon the material energy.
His expansion as the primordial sound vibration.
His consorting with the divine energy, Yoganidrā.
questions.correct_answer: His thousands of thousands of subjective portions.
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that Mahā-Viṣṇu manifests these avatāras in His thousands of thousands of subjective portions.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the description of Mahā-Viṣṇu emphasize the vast scale of His transcendental potency?
options: By repeating the phrase 'thousands of thousands' regarding His heads, eyes, hands, avatāras, and souls.
By describing His size as encompassing the entire Causal Ocean.
By focusing on His single, all-encompassing cosmic form.
By comparing His limbs to the mountains and rivers of the material world.
questions.correct_answer: By repeating the phrase 'thousands of thousands' regarding His heads, eyes, hands, avatāras, and souls.
questions.explanation: The verse uses the repetitive quantifier 'thousands of thousands' across all the attributes mentioned, highlighting the infinite nature of His work and existence.

12.verse_text: The same Mahā-Viṣṇu is spoken of by the name of "Nārāyaṇa" in this mundane world. From that eternal person has sprung the vast expanse of water of the spiritual Causal Ocean. The subjective portion of Saṅkarṣaṇa who abides in paravyoma, the above supreme puruṣa with thousands of subjective portions, reposes in the state of divine sleep [yoga-nidrā] in the waters of the spiritual Causal Ocean.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the alternative name by which the Lord of the mundane world, Mahā-Viṣṇu, is known within this material realm?
options: Nārāyaṇa
Vāsudeva
Pradyumna
Aniruddha
questions.correct_answer: Nārāyaṇa
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the same Mahā-Viṣṇu is referred to by the name Nārāyaṇa in this mundane world.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the name of the divine sleep in which the supreme puruṣa, Mahā-Viṣṇu, reposes while resting in the waters of the Causal Ocean?
options: Yoga-nidrā
Viṣṇu-nidrā
Kāraṇa-nidrā
Ananta-nidrā
questions.correct_answer: Yoga-nidrā
questions.explanation: The text describes Mahā-Viṣṇu as reposing in the state of divine sleep known as yoga-nidrā within the spiritual Causal Ocean.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, from which eternal personage has the vast expanse of the spiritual Causal Ocean originated?
options: Mahā-Viṣṇu
Kṣīrodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
Garbhodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
Saṅkarṣaṇa
questions.correct_answer: Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the vast expanse of the water of the spiritual Causal Ocean has sprung from Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which subjective portion of the Lord resides in the paravyoma while the supreme puruṣa reposes in the Causal Ocean?
options: Saṅkarṣaṇa
Vāsudeva
Pradyumna
Aniruddha
questions.correct_answer: Saṅkarṣaṇa
questions.explanation: The text identifies the subjective portion of Saṅkarṣaṇa as the one who abides in the paravyoma while Mahā-Viṣṇu rests in divine sleep.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, where is the supreme puruṣa, Mahā-Viṣṇu, described as reposing during His state of divine slumber?
options: In the waters of the spiritual Causal Ocean
In the effulgence of the Brahmanda
Upon the bed of Ananta-śeṣa in Vaikuṇṭha
Within the hexagonal whorl of Gokula
questions.correct_answer: In the waters of the spiritual Causal Ocean
questions.explanation: The text clearly states that the supreme puruṣa reposes in the waters of the spiritual Causal Ocean.

13.verse_text: The spiritual seeds of Saṅkarṣaṇa existing in the pores of skin of Mahā-Viṣṇu, are born as so many golden sperms. These sperms are covered with five great elements.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific nature and color of the seeds that manifest from the pores of the skin of Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: White diamonds
Golden sperms
Blue lotus petals
Red rubies
questions.correct_answer: Golden sperms
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies that the spiritual seeds originating from the pores of Mahā-Viṣṇu's skin are born as golden sperms.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what covering do the golden sperms that spring from the pores of Mahā-Viṣṇu possess?
options: The three modes of material nature
The five great elements
The ten directions of space
The seven layers of the cosmic shell
questions.correct_answer: The five great elements
questions.explanation: The scripture states that these golden sperms are specifically covered with the five great elements (pancha-mahabhuta).

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the origin of the golden sperms that eventually develop into universal seeds?
options: The breath of Kāraṇārṇavaśāyī Viṣṇu
The pores of the skin of Mahā-Viṣṇu
The lotus flower arising from Garbhodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
The effulgence of the spiritual sky
questions.correct_answer: The pores of the skin of Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly attributes the origin of these golden sperms to the pores found on the body of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, the spiritual seeds associated with whom are manifest as golden sperms?
options: Aniruddha
Saṅkarṣaṇa
Pradyumna
Vasudeva
questions.correct_answer: Saṅkarṣaṇa
questions.explanation: The verse identifies these seeds as the spiritual seeds of Saṅkarṣaṇa existing within the pores of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what term describes the initial state of the seeds that emerge from the pores of Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: Spiritual seeds
Material atoms
Time fragments
Causal vibrations
questions.correct_answer: Spiritual seeds
questions.explanation: The text refers to these entities as spiritual seeds of Saṅkarṣaṇa that subsequently become golden sperms.

14.verse_text: The same Mahā-Viṣṇu entered into each universe as His own separate subjective portions. The divine portions, that entered into each universe are possessed of His majestic extension, i.e., they are the eternal universal soul Mahā-Viṣṇu, possessing thousands of thousands of heads.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the supreme Mahā-Viṣṇu manifest within each individual universe?
options: By manifesting His own separate subjective portions
By manifesting as the aggregate of all demigods
By sending the golden sperms of Saṅkarṣaṇa
By expanding as the causal ocean itself
questions.correct_answer: By manifesting His own separate subjective portions
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Mahā-Viṣṇu enters into each universe as His own separate subjective portions.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific transcendental attribute characterizes the portions of Mahā-Viṣṇu that enter each universe?
options: They are possessed of His majestic extension
They are limited by the five great elements
They are identical to the form of Śambhu
They are veiled by the influence of Yoganidrā
questions.correct_answer: They are possessed of His majestic extension
questions.explanation: The verses describe these subjective portions as being possessed of His majestic extension, identifying them as the eternal universal soul.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the internal nature of the universal soul, Mahā-Viṣṇu, who enters each universe, described?
options: Possessing thousands of thousands of heads
Residing eternally within the Causal Ocean
Manifesting as the four-handed form of Vasudeva
Appearing as a golden youth of infinite size
questions.correct_answer: Possessing thousands of thousands of heads
questions.explanation: The text describes these divine portions as the eternal universal soul, Mahā-Viṣṇu, who possesses thousands of thousands of heads.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what title is given to the divine portions of Mahā-Viṣṇu that enter into each individual universe?
options: The eternal universal soul
The progenitors of the material elements
The source of the transcendental effulgence
The guardians of the cardinal directions
questions.correct_answer: The eternal universal soul
questions.explanation: The text identifies the divine portions that enter each universe as the eternal universal soul, Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, to what degree does the entry of Mahā-Viṣṇu into the universes reflect His supreme nature?
options: As separate subjective portions of His own
As a diminished expansion of His original potency
As a transformation of the five great elements
As the final manifestation of the shadow of cit-potency
questions.correct_answer: As separate subjective portions of His own
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Mahā-Viṣṇu remains unified in nature even while entering each universe as separate subjective portions.

15.verse_text: The same Mahā-Viṣṇu created Viṣṇu from His left limb, Brahmā, the first progenitor of beings, from His right limb and, from the space between His two eyebrows, Śambhu, the divine masculine manifested halo.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, from which specific part of Mahā-Viṣṇu’s transcendental form is Lord Brahmā, the first progenitor of beings, manifested?
options: From the space between His two eyebrows
From His right limb
From His left limb
From the lotus of His heart
questions.correct_answer: From His right limb
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Brahmā is created from the right limb of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which divine personality is manifested from the space between the two eyebrows of Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: Lord Brahmā
Lord Śiva (Śambhu)
Lord Viṣṇu
Kṣīrodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: Lord Śiva (Śambhu)
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that Śambhu, described as the divine masculine manifested halo, emerges from the space between the two eyebrows of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the manifestation of the Lord known as Viṣṇu described in relation to Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: He is created from the right limb
He is created from the space between the eyebrows
He is created from the left limb
He is created from the breath of Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: He is created from the left limb
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Mahā-Viṣṇu creates Viṣṇu from His left limb.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the nature of Śambhu characterized at the moment of His manifestation?
options: As the primordial sound of the Vedas
As the divine masculine manifested halo
As the shelter of all jīvas
As the pure cit potency of the Lord
questions.correct_answer: As the divine masculine manifested halo
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Śambhu specifically as the divine masculine manifested halo emerging from the brow-center of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which entity is identified as the first progenitor of all beings who is manifested from the right limb of Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: Śambhu
Viṣṇu
Brahmā
Sadasiva
questions.correct_answer: Brahmā
questions.explanation: The text identifies Brahmā as the first progenitor and specifies that he originates from the right limb of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

16.verse_text: The function of Śambhu in relation to jīvas is that this universe enshrining the mundane egotistic principle has originated from Śambhu.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific function of Śambhu regarding the jīvas and the material existence?
options: He directly creates the spiritual Vaikuṇṭha planets
He is the source of the universe that enshrines the mundane egotistic principle
He maintains the internal potency of the Supreme Lord
He protects the jīvas from the influence of Yoganidrā
questions.correct_answer: He is the source of the universe that enshrines the mundane egotistic principle
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the function of Śambhu in relation to jīvas is that this universe, which enshrines the mundane egotistic principle, originates from him.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what foundational principle is associated with the universe brought forth by Śambhu?
options: The pure consciousness of the cit-potency
The mundane egotistic principle
The transcendental effulgence of Goloka
The eternal bliss of Yoganidrā
questions.correct_answer: The mundane egotistic principle
questions.explanation: The verse clearly links the universe created by Śambhu to the enshrinement of the mundane egotistic principle.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the relationship between the jīvas and Śambhu characterized in terms of their existential environment?
options: They are liberated within the divine halo of the Lord
They exist within a universe originating from Śambhu that is grounded in egotism
They reside in the transcendental petals of the lotus of Goloka
They are directly maintained by the golden sperms of Sankarṣaṇa
questions.correct_answer: They exist within a universe originating from Śambhu that is grounded in egotism
questions.explanation: The scripture defines the connection between Śambhu and the jīvas by identifying him as the origin of the universe of mundane egotism.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what role does Śambhu play within the structure of the cosmic manifestation?
options: He acts as the direct provider of the eighteen-syllable mantra
He serves as the origin for the mundane realm defined by egotistic principle
He is the final destination for souls seeking liberation
He guards the entrance to the Causal Ocean
questions.correct_answer: He serves as the origin for the mundane realm defined by egotistic principle
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that the universe enshrining mundane egotism originates from Śambhu.

questions.question: Based on the teachings of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, why is the universe associated with Śambhu considered distinct in its nature?
options: Because it is filled with the spiritual light of Svetadvipa
Because it enshrines the mundane egotistic principle
Because it is the abode where Yoganidrā resides in pure cit potency
Because it is constructed from the jewels of the transcendental world
questions.correct_answer: Because it enshrines the mundane egotistic principle
questions.explanation: The specific descriptor provided in the text for the universe originating from Śambhu is that it enshrines the mundane egotistic principle.

17.verse_text: Thereupon the same great personal Godhead, assuming the threefold forms of Viṣṇu, Prajāpati and Śambhu, entering into the mundane universe, plays the pastimes of preservation, creation and destruction of this world. This pastime is contained in the mundane world. Hence, it being perverted, the Supreme Lord, identical with Mahā-Viṣṇu, prefers to consort with the goddess Yoganidrā, the constituent of His own spiritual [cit] potency full of the ecstatic trance of eternal bliss appertaining to His own divine personality.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what are the three specific forms assumed by the Supreme Personality of Godhead when entering the mundane universe to oversee its maintenance and dissolution?
options: Viṣṇu, Prajāpati, and Śambhu
Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Maheśvara
Kṛṣṇa, Balarāma, and Aniruddha
Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, and Pradyumna
questions.correct_answer: Viṣṇu, Prajāpati, and Śambhu
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the Supreme Godhead assumes the threefold forms of Viṣṇu, Prajāpati, and Śambhu to perform the pastimes of preservation, creation, and destruction.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, with whom does the Supreme Lord, appearing as Mahā-Viṣṇu, prefer to consort while maintaining the pastimes of this mundane world?
options: Lakṣmī-devī
Yoganidrā
Rādhārāṇī
Durgā-devī
questions.correct_answer: Yoganidrā
questions.explanation: The text states that because the mundane pastime is perverted, the Supreme Lord, identical with Mahā-Viṣṇu, prefers to consort with the goddess Yoganidrā, who is a constituent of His own spiritual potency.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the internal nature of the goddess Yoganidrā, with whom the Lord consorts in the mundane universe?
options: A manifestation of the illusory energy
A constituent of His own spiritual cit potency
The personification of the Vedas
A secondary expansion of the material elements
questions.correct_answer: A constituent of His own spiritual cit potency
questions.explanation: The text defines Yoganidrā as the constituent of the Lord's own spiritual (cit) potency, full of the ecstatic trance of eternal bliss.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which specific cosmic pastimes are performed by the threefold forms of the Godhead within the mundane universe?
options: Creation, sustenance, and expansion
Preservation, creation, and destruction
Birth, growth, and liberation
Manifestation, expansion, and withdrawal
questions.correct_answer: Preservation, creation, and destruction
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly attributes the functions of preservation, creation, and destruction to the threefold forms of Viṣṇu, Prajāpati, and Śambhu.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what characterizes the state of the mundane pastimes of the Lord, prompting Him to consort with Yoganidrā?
options: They are considered absolute and perfect
They are perverted manifestations
They are eternal in the spiritual world
They are merely mental projections of Brahmā
questions.correct_answer: They are perverted manifestations
questions.explanation: The text notes that because the mundane pastime is perverted, the Lord chooses to associate with Yoganidrā to maintain His divine personality in that realm.

18.verse_text: When Viṣṇu lying in the ocean of milk wills to create this universe, a golden lotus springs from His navel-pit. The golden lotus with its stem is the abode of Brahmā representing Brahmaloka or Satyaloka.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific entity emerges from the navel-pit of Viṣṇu when He desires to manifest the universe?
options: A golden lotus
The primordial causal ocean
The manifestation of Mahā-Māyā
A shower of celestial nectar
questions.correct_answer: A golden lotus
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that when Viṣṇu wills to create the universe, a golden lotus springs from His navel-pit.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the significance of the stem associated with the golden lotus?
options: It serves as the abode of Brahmā
It connects the material world to the Causal Ocean
It represents the path of the soul returning to Vaikunṭha
It forms the axis of the seven planetary systems
questions.correct_answer: It serves as the abode of Brahmā
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies the golden lotus, along with its stem, as the abode of Brahmā, representing Brahmaloka or Satyaloka.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is another name given to the abode of Brahmā which originates from the navel of Viṣṇu?
options: Satyaloka
Janaloka
Tapoloka
Maharloka
questions.correct_answer: Satyaloka
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the abode of Brahmā created from the navel lotus is known as Brahmaloka or Satyaloka.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which specific deity lies within the ocean of milk during the initial phases of cosmic creation?
options: Viṣṇu
Śambhu
Maha-Viṣṇu
Garbhodakasayī Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The text specifies that it is Viṣṇu who, while lying in the ocean of milk, wills the creation of the universe.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, the golden lotus serves as the seat for which creator deity?
options: Brahmā
Indra
Vāyu
Varuṇa
questions.correct_answer: Brahmā
questions.explanation: The text states the golden lotus is the abode of Brahmā, where the secondary creation takes place.

19.verse_text: Before their conglomeration the primary elements in their nascent state remained originally separate entities. Nonapplication of the conglomerating process is the cause of their separate existence. Divine Mahā-Viṣṇu, primal Godhead, through association with His own spiritual [cit] potency, moved Māyā and by the application of the conglomerating principle created those different entities in their state of cooperation. And after that He Himself consorted with Yoganidrā by way of His eternal dalliance with His spiritual [cit] potency.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary cause for the primary elements of the universe remaining as separate entities before the process of conglomeration?
options: The nonapplication of the conglomerating process
The influence of the three modes of material nature
The separation caused by the ego of the jīvas
The absence of the interaction between Puruṣa and Prakṛti
questions.correct_answer: The nonapplication of the conglomerating process
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the nonapplication of the conglomerating process is the reason the primary elements remained originally separate entities.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does Mahā-Viṣṇu initiate the process of creating the different entities in a state of cooperation?
options: By directly manifesting His own bodily effulgence
Through association with His own spiritual cit potency and moving Māyā
By breathing out the lords of mundane worlds
By meditating upon the form of Govinda
questions.correct_answer: Through association with His own spiritual cit potency and moving Māyā
questions.explanation: The verse states that Mahā-Viṣṇu, through association with His own spiritual cit potency, moved Māyā and applied the conglomerating principle to create the entities.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the consorting of Mahā-Viṣṇu after the creation of the different entities?
options: He consorts with the goddess of fortune, Lakṣmī
He consorts with Yoganidrā by way of eternal dalliance with His spiritual cit potency
He consorts with the material energy known as Durgā
He consorts with the expansion of Sankarṣaṇa
questions.correct_answer: He consorts with Yoganidrā by way of eternal dalliance with His spiritual cit potency
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that after the creation, Mahā-Viṣṇu Himself consorted with Yoganidrā, which is a form of His eternal dalliance with His spiritual cit potency.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the original state of the primary elements before they are organized into a functioning universe?
options: They are unified in the body of Kāraṇodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
They exist as separate, individual entities
They are dissolved into the impersonal Brahman
They are held within the womb of the material nature
questions.correct_answer: They are held within the womb of the material nature
questions.explanation: The verse states that before the conglomeration occurred, the primary elements in their nascent state remained as originally separate entities.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what principle does Mahā-Viṣṇu apply to cause the separate elements to cooperate in the creation of the universe?
options: The principle of eternal time
The principle of conglomeration
The principle of expansion
The principle of divine desire
questions.correct_answer: The principle of conglomeration
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that by the application of the conglomerating principle, Mahā-Viṣṇu created the different entities in their state of cooperation.

20.verse_text: By conglomerating all those separate entities He manifested the innumerable mundane universes and Himself entered into the inmost recess of every extended conglomerate [viraḍ-vigraha]. At that time those jīvas who had lain dormant during the cataclysm were awakened.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what does Mahā-Viṣṇu manifest by conglomerating all the separate primary elements?
options: The innumerable mundane universes
The transcendental Vaikuṇṭha planets
The internal potency of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
The eternal abode of Goloka
questions.correct_answer: The innumerable mundane universes
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that by conglomerating the separate entities, Mahā-Viṣṇu manifested the innumerable mundane universes.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, where does Mahā-Viṣṇu enter after manifesting the cosmic conglomerates?
options: Into the inmost recess of every extended conglomerate
Into the hearts of the liberated sages
Into the spiritual effulgence of Brahman
Into the lotus of Brahmā's heart
questions.correct_answer: Into the inmost recess of every extended conglomerate
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Mahā-Viṣṇu Himself enters into the inmost recess of every extended conglomerate, known as the virāḍ-vigraha.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what state were the jīvas in before Mahā-Viṣṇu manifested the universes?
options: Dormant during the cataclysm
Active in the spiritual sky
Engaged in devotional service
Existing as pure spirit souls
questions.correct_answer: Dormant during the cataclysm
questions.explanation: The scripture describes that those jīvas who had been lying dormant during the cataclysm were awakened upon the manifestation of the universes.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what happens to the dormant jīvas when the mundane universes are created?
options: They are awakened
They are dissolved into Brahman
They are sent to Vaikuṇṭha
They are transformed into matter
questions.correct_answer: They are awakened
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that at the time of the manifestation of the universes, those jīvas who had lain dormant during the cataclysm were awakened.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the term used to describe the extended conglomerate which Mahā-Viṣṇu enters?
options: Virāḍ-vigraha
Caitanya-līlā
Yoganidrā-tattva
Cit-śakti
questions.correct_answer: Virāḍ-vigraha
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies the extended conglomerate that Mahā-Viṣṇu enters as the virāḍ-vigraha.

21.verse_text: The same jīva is eternal and is for eternity and without a beginning joined to the Supreme Lord by the tie of an eternal kinship. He is transcendental spiritual potency.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the relationship between the eternal jīva and the Supreme Lord best described?
options: A connection based on karma and previous pious deeds
A temporary bond severed by material illusion
A tie of eternal kinship that is without beginning
A contractual agreement established through Vedic rituals
questions.correct_answer: A tie of eternal kinship that is without beginning
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the jīva is eternal and is for eternity joined to the Supreme Lord by the tie of an eternal kinship without a beginning.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the fundamental nature of the jīva in relation to its potency?
options: The jīva is a manifestation of material energy
The jīva is transcendental spiritual potency
The jīva is a fragment of the illusory energy of Durga
The jīva is a reflection of the demigod's power
questions.correct_answer: The jīva is transcendental spiritual potency
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the jīva itself is transcendental spiritual potency.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the temporal status of the jīva's existence and its connection to the Supreme?
options: It begins upon the jīva's entry into the material world
It is for eternity and without a beginning
It only exists when the jīva is liberated
It is established after the creation of the universes
questions.correct_answer: It is for eternity and without a beginning
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the jīva is eternal and its link to the Supreme Lord is for eternity and without a beginning.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the bond linking the soul to Govinda?
options: An artificial veil of material nature
An eternal kinship that transcends material time
A conditional state requiring constant purification
A transitory experience of the living entity
questions.correct_answer: An eternal kinship that transcends material time
questions.explanation: The text characterizes the connection as a tie of eternal kinship that has no beginning, indicating it is beyond material time.

questions.question: Based on the wisdom of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which statement accurately reflects the status of the jīva?
options: The jīva is the origin of the Supreme Lord's material creation
The jīva is inherently joined to the Supreme Lord eternally
The jīva is merely a spectator of the divine drama
The jīva is a product of the interactions of the three modes of nature
questions.correct_answer: The jīva is inherently joined to the Supreme Lord eternally
questions.explanation: The verse emphasizes that the jīva is for eternity joined to the Supreme Lord.

22.verse_text: The divine lotus which springs from the navel-pit of Viṣṇu is in every way related by the spiritual tie with all souls and is the origin of four-faced Brahmā versed in the four Vedas.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate source of the divine lotus that serves as the birth-site for the four-faced Brahmā?
options: The causal ocean of Maha-Visnu
The navel-pit of Visnu
The spiritual aura of Goloka
The thousand-petaled lotus of the heart
questions.correct_answer: The navel-pit of Visnu
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the divine lotus, which is the origin of the four-faced Brahma, springs specifically from the navel-pit of Visnu.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the relationship between the divine lotus and all jiva souls described?
options: It acts as a temporary vehicle for material bondage
It is unrelated to the condition of the individual soul
It is related in every way by a spiritual tie
It serves as a prison for souls desiring to leave Vaikuntha
questions.correct_answer: It is related in every way by a spiritual tie
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that the lotus originating from the Lord is in every way related by a spiritual tie with all souls.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific scriptural knowledge does the four-faced Brahmā possess upon his manifestation from the lotus?
options: The six Vedangas
The four Vedas
The eighteen Puranas
The hundred and eight Upanisads
questions.correct_answer: The four Vedas
questions.explanation: The text identifies the entity born from the lotus as the four-faced Brahma, who is inherently versed in the four Vedas.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary structural origin for the four-faced creator of the universe?
options: A divine lotus
A golden egg
A pillar of light
A transcendental conch
questions.correct_answer: A divine lotus
questions.explanation: The verse states that the divine lotus springs from the navel-pit of Visnu and serves as the origin of Brahma.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the fundamental nature of the connection between the lotus, the Lord, and the souls as mentioned in the context of Brahma's origin?
options: A material causal connection
An eternal spiritual tie
A temporary illusionary construct
A distant karmic association
questions.correct_answer: An eternal spiritual tie
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the divine lotus is related to all souls by a spiritual tie, emphasizing the transcendental origin of the cosmic creator.

23.verse_text: On coming out of the lotus, Brahmā, being guided by the divine potency tuned his mind to the act of creation under the impulse of previous impressions. But he could see nothing but darkness in every direction.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what did Lord Brahmā perceive when he first emerged from the divine lotus and prepared himself for the act of creation?
options: A brilliant effulgence emanating from the Causal Ocean
The universal form of the Lord showing all planets
Nothing but darkness in every direction
The transcendental beauty of the residents of Vaikuntha
questions.correct_answer: Nothing but darkness in every direction
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that upon coming out of the lotus, Brahmā could see nothing but darkness in every direction.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what internal impulse guided Lord Brahmā as he tuned his mind toward the task of creation?
options: The desire to become the master of the material worlds
The impulse of previous impressions
Direct commands whispered by the four Vedas
The exhaustion felt after his long sleep on the lotus
questions.correct_answer: The impulse of previous impressions
questions.explanation: The verse notes that Brahmā was guided by divine potency to tune his mind toward creation specifically under the impulse of his previous impressions.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific factor allowed Lord Brahmā to begin the act of creation despite his surroundings?
options: His inherent knowledge of the four Vedas
The guidance of the divine potency
The presence of the demigods waiting for instructions
The illuminating light of the spiritual sun
questions.correct_answer: The guidance of the divine potency
questions.explanation: The scriptural text mentions that it was the divine potency that guided Brahmā as he tuned his mind to the act of creation.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what was the primary source of Brahmā's inspiration before he received the eighteen-syllable mantra?
options: The reflection of the Lord's form in the Causal Ocean
The sounds of the flute echoing through the lotus
Previous impressions within his consciousness
Direct visions of the spiritual realm of Goloka
questions.correct_answer: Previous impressions within his consciousness
questions.explanation: The text describes Brahmā's state after emerging from the lotus as being influenced by previous impressions while facing universal darkness.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the scriptural account characterize the environment encountered by Brahmā immediately after his birth from the lotus?
options: Full of the golden radiance of the Lord's effulgence
Filled with the chaos of the material elements
Total gloom and darkness in all directions
The peaceful stillness of the transcendental abode
questions.correct_answer: Total gloom and darkness in all directions
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that Brahmā initially saw nothing but darkness in every direction when he emerged from the lotus.

24.verse_text: Then the goddess of learning Sarasvatī, the divine consort of the Supreme Lord, said thus to Brahmā who saw nothing but gloom in all directions, "O Brahmā, this mantra, viz., klīṁ kṛṣṇāya govindāya gopī-jana-vallabhāya svāhā, will assuredly fulfill your heart's desire."

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, when Lord Brahmā emerged from the lotus and perceived only darkness in every direction, which divine personality appeared to grant him the path to creative fulfillment?
options: The goddess of learning, Sarasvatī
The personified potency, Yoganidrā
The eternal consort, Śrī Rādhikā
The goddess of fortune, Lakṣmī-devī
questions.correct_answer: The goddess of learning, Sarasvatī
questions.explanation: The text states that the goddess of learning, Sarasvatī, the divine consort of the Supreme Lord, spoke to Brahmā when he saw only gloom in all directions.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific eighteen-syllable mantra that the goddess of learning instructed Lord Brahmā to meditate upon to fulfill his heart's desire?
options: Oṁ namo bhagavate vāsudevāya
Klīṁ kṛṣṇāya govindāya gopī-jana-vallabhāya svāhā
Hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare
Gopī-jana-vallabhāya namo namaḥ
questions.correct_answer: Klīṁ kṛṣṇāya govindāya gopī-jana-vallabhāya svāhā
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies this eighteen-syllable mantra as the one given by Sarasvatī to Brahmā to fulfill his desires.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the goddess of learning, Sarasvatī, described in relation to the Supreme Lord during her interaction with Lord Brahmā?
options: As the shadow of the external potency
As the divine consort of the Supreme Lord
As the personification of the causal ocean
As the guardian of the Vaikuṇṭha gate
questions.correct_answer: As the divine consort of the Supreme Lord
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly addresses Sarasvatī as the divine consort of the Supreme Lord when she approaches Brahmā.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what was the state of the universe when the goddess of learning instructed Lord Brahmā to utilize the transcendental mantra?
options: It was filled with the effulgence of Vaikuṇṭha
It was manifesting the various planetary systems
It was characterized by darkness or gloom in every direction
It was vibrant with the sound of the flute
questions.correct_answer: It was characterized by darkness or gloom in every direction
questions.explanation: The verse notes that Sarasvatī spoke to Brahmā at the moment he saw nothing but gloom in all directions.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the intended purpose of the mantra provided to Lord Brahmā by the goddess of learning?
options: To destroy the material demons
To fulfill the desires of his heart
To manifest the material demigods
To reveal the architecture of Svetadvīpa
questions.correct_answer: To fulfill the desires of his heart
questions.explanation: The goddess of learning explicitly tells Brahmā that the mantra will assuredly fulfill his heart's desire.

25.verse_text: "O Brahmā, do thou practice spiritual association by means of this mantra; then all your desires will be fulfilled."

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific instruction did the goddess of learning, Sarasvatī, give to Lord Brahmā when he perceived only darkness in all directions?
options: She initiated him into the eighteen-syllable mantra to fulfill his desires
She commanded him to meditate upon the form of the Universal Form
She advised him to perform a great sacrifice on the banks of the Virajā
She instructed him to seek the guidance of the four Kumaras
questions.correct_answer: She initiated him into the eighteen-syllable mantra to fulfill his desires
questions.explanation: Sarasvatī appeared to Brahmā in the midst of gloom and provided the mantra klīṁ kṛṣṇāya govindāya gopī-jana-vallabhāya svāhā to ensure the fulfillment of his heart's desire.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate result of practicing spiritual association through the specific mantra given to Lord Brahmā?
options: One attains the position of Indra in the heavenly planets
One becomes freed from the cycle of birth and death forever
All the inner desires of the practitioner will be fulfilled
One attains the mystic power of creating various universes
questions.correct_answer: All the inner desires of the practitioner will be fulfilled
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that by practicing spiritual association through this mantra, all of Brahmā's desires would be fulfilled.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what divine configuration does the transcendental land of Goloka contain, which Lord Brahmā visualized during his meditation?
options: A magnificent golden palace with countless towers
A divine lotus of a thousand petals augmented by millions of filaments
A vast ocean of nectar surrounding a diamond island
A series of seven concentric circles of different elements
questions.correct_answer: A divine lotus of a thousand petals augmented by millions of filaments
questions.explanation: Brahmā meditated upon a divine lotus of a thousand petals with millions of filaments situated in the transcendental land of Goloka.
