questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who is seated upon the great divine throne located on the whorl of the transcendental lotus in Goloka?
options: Lord Nārāyaṇa of Vaikuṇṭha
Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the form of eternal effulgence of transcendental bliss
Lord Śiva in his form as Mahākāla
The primordial Puruṣa-avatāra
questions.correct_answer: Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the form of eternal effulgence of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The text describes Śrī Kṛṣṇa as the one seated on the divine throne upon the whorl of the lotus, possessing a form of eternal effulgence and transcendental bliss.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who are the devotees engaged in the worship of Lord Kṛṣṇa on the divine throne within Goloka?
options: The various demigods headed by Indra
The great sages headed by Sanaka-kumāra
His amorous milkmaids along with their subjective portions and extensions
The residents of the various heavenly planetary systems
questions.correct_answer: His amorous milkmaids along with their subjective portions and extensions
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that Kṛṣṇa is worshiped in Goloka by His amorous milkmaids with their respective subjective portions and extensions, as well as by the external energy.

26.verse_text: Brahmā, being desirous of satisfying Govinda, practiced the cultural acts for Kṛṣṇa in Goloka, Lord of Śvetadvīpa, for a long time. His meditation ran thus, "There exists a divine lotus of a thousand petals, augmented by millions of filaments, in the transcendental land of Goloka. On its whorl, there exists a great divine throne on which is seated Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the form of eternal effulgence of transcendental bliss, playing on His divine flute resonant with the divine sound, with His lotus mouth. He is worshiped by His amorous milkmaids with their respective subjective portions and extensions and also by His external energy [who stays outside] embodying all mundane qualities."

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific nature of the divine lotus situated within the transcendental land of Goloka where Śrī Kṛṣṇa resides?
options: A lotus of a thousand petals augmented by millions of filaments
A hundred-petaled lotus representing the Vedas
A lotus made of pure gold with endless radiant stems
A divine blue lotus containing the entire cosmic manifestation
questions.correct_answer: A lotus of a thousand petals augmented by millions of filaments
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes the divine lotus in Goloka as having a thousand petals and millions of filaments.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who is observed to be worshiping Śrī Kṛṣṇa as He sits upon the divine throne within the whorl of the lotus?
options: The four Kumaras and the sages
The demigods of the heavenly planets
His amorous milkmaids with their subjective portions and extensions
The personified Vedas and the Upanisads
questions.correct_answer: His amorous milkmaids with their subjective portions and extensions
questions.explanation: The verse states that Krsna is worshipped by His amorous milkmaids (gopis) along with their subjective portions and extensions.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary activity of Śrī Kṛṣṇa while He is seated upon the divine throne in the whorl of the transcendental lotus?
options: He is engaged in the creation of the material universes
He is playing on His divine flute, resonant with divine sound
He is reciting the Vedic hymns to the assembly of sages
He is resting in the Causal Ocean of transcendental bliss
questions.correct_answer: He is playing on His divine flute, resonant with divine sound
questions.explanation: The scripture describes Krsna playing on His divine flute, which resonates with divine sound from His lotus mouth.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the external energy of the Lord positioned in relation to the divine lotus of Goloka?
options: She resides within the central filaments of the lotus
She stays outside, embodying all mundane qualities
She is hidden beneath the thousand petals
She sits upon a secondary throne near the Lord
questions.correct_answer: She stays outside, embodying all mundane qualities
questions.explanation: The verse notes that the external energy stays outside the divine lotus while the inner transcendental pastimes occur.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what form is Śrī Kṛṣṇa described as possessing when He is seated upon the great divine throne in Goloka?
options: The form of eternal effulgence of transcendental bliss
The form of the universal manifestation
The form of the four-armed Narayana
The form of the unmanifested Brahman
questions.correct_answer: The form of eternal effulgence of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The text specifically characterizes Krsna's form while on the throne as the eternal effulgence of transcendental bliss.

27.verse_text: Then Gāyatrī, mother of the Vedas, being made manifest, i.e. imparted, by the divine sound of the flute of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, entered into the lotus mouth of Brahmā, born from himself, through his eight ear-holes. The lotus-born Brahmā having received the Gāyatrī, sprung from the flute-song of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, attained the status of the twice-born, having been initiated by the supreme primal preceptor, Godhead Himself.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how did the Gāyatrī mantra first manifest for Lord Brahmā?
options: It emerged from the lotus flower of the Causal Ocean
It was imparted by the divine sound of the flute of Śrī Kṛṣṇa
It was whispered by the personified Vedas into his heart
It appeared written in golden letters upon the thousand-petaled lotus
questions.correct_answer: It was imparted by the divine sound of the flute of Śrī Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the Gāyatrī, the mother of the Vedas, was made manifest by the divine sound of the flute of Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, through which specific entry point did the Gāyatrī mantra enter into Lord Brahmā?
options: His lotus heart
His two eyes
His eight ear-holes
His crown chakra
questions.correct_answer: His eight ear-holes
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the Gāyatrī entered into the lotus mouth of Brahmā through his eight ear-holes.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what transformation occurred for Lord Brahmā upon receiving the Gāyatrī?
options: He became the creator of the material universe
He attained the status of the twice-born
He received the vision of the transcendental form of Govinda
He was empowered to manifest the four Vedas
questions.correct_answer: He attained the status of the twice-born
questions.explanation: The text states that after receiving the Gāyatrī, Brahmā attained the status of the twice-born, having been initiated by the Supreme Lord.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who acted as the supreme primal preceptor to initiate Lord Brahmā?
options: The personified Vedas
Lord Śiva
Godhead Himself, Śrī Kṛṣṇa
The Goddess of Learning, Sarasvatī
questions.correct_answer: Godhead Himself, Śrī Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that Brahmā was initiated by the supreme primal preceptor, who is Godhead Himself.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what title is given to the Gāyatrī mantra that entered into Lord Brahmā?
options: The essence of the Upanisads
The crown of the Mantras
The mother of the Vedas
The vibration of the spiritual sky
questions.correct_answer: The mother of the Vedas
questions.explanation: The text identifies Gāyatrī as the mother of the Vedas at the moment of her manifestation from Kṛṣṇa's flute.

28.verse_text: Enlightened by the recollection of that Gāyatrī, embodying the three Vedas, Brahmā became acquainted with the expanse of the ocean of truth. Then he worshiped Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the essence of all Vedas, with this hymn.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific internal empowerment did Brahmā receive that allowed him to become acquainted with the expanse of the ocean of truth?
options: The chanting of the eighteen-syllable mantra
The recollection of the Gāyatrī
The vision of the hexagonal whorl of Goloka
The touch of the dust from Govinda’s lotus feet
questions.correct_answer: The recollection of the Gāyatrī
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that enlightened by the recollection of that Gāyatrī, Brahmā became acquainted with the expanse of the ocean of truth.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, the Gāyatrī that enlightened Brahmā is described as embodying what specific spiritual entity?
options: The Supreme personality of Godhead
The energy of the internal potency
The three Vedas
The essence of the Causal Ocean
questions.correct_answer: The three Vedas
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that the Gāyatrī which enlightened Brahmā is described as embodying the three Vedas.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, after attaining enlightenment through the Gāyatrī, whom did Brahmā worship as the very essence of all the Vedas?
options: Nārāyaṇa
Śrī Kṛṣṇa
Maha-Viṣṇu
Sankarṣaṇa
questions.correct_answer: Śrī Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The verse states that after becoming acquainted with the ocean of truth, Brahmā worshiped Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who is the essence of all Vedas.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what was the primary act of devotion performed by Brahmā once he understood the truth of the Vedas?
options: He began the creation of the material universes
He offered a hymn of worship to the Supreme Lord
He performed rigorous penance in the dark void
He meditated upon the form of the four-armed Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: He offered a hymn of worship to the Supreme Lord
questions.explanation: The text describes that Brahmā, having realized the truth, worshiped Śrī Kṛṣṇa with a hymn.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the prerequisite state of consciousness mentioned for Brahmā to recognize the essence of the Vedas?
options: Deep trance in the Causal Ocean
Recollection of the Gāyatrī
Guidance from the demigods
Direct vision of the form of Govinda
questions.correct_answer: Recollection of the Gāyatrī
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that it was the recollection of the Gāyatrī that served as the catalyst for Brahmā’s enlightenment and subsequent worship of Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

29.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, the first progenitor who is tending the cows, yielding all desire, in abodes built with spiritual gems, surrounded by millions of purpose trees, always served with great reverence and affection by hundreds of thousands of lakṣmīs or gopīs.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific occupation is the primeval Lord Govinda described as performing within His transcendental abode?
options: Playing the flute for the gopīs
Tending the cows
Governing the affairs of the material universes
Resting upon the bed of Ananta-śeṣa
questions.correct_answer: Tending the cows
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Govinda is the first progenitor who is tending the cows, yielding all desire.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the abodes in which the primeval Lord Govinda resides?
options: Abodes constructed of eternal golden light
Abodes built with spiritual gems
Abodes formed from the milk of the ocean of truth
Abodes manifested by the shadow of the cit potency
questions.correct_answer: Abodes built with spiritual gems
questions.explanation: The text describes Govinda's abode as being built with spiritual gems, highlighting the transcendental opulence of His home.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what surrounds the transcendental abode of the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: Millions of wish-fulfilling or purpose trees
The dense waters of the Causal Ocean
An assembly of sages chanting the Vedas
The protective tridents of the dik-palas
questions.correct_answer: Millions of wish-fulfilling or purpose trees
questions.explanation: The verse mentions that the Lord's abode is surrounded by millions of purpose trees (kalpa-druma).

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who serves the primeval Lord Govinda with great reverence and affection?
options: The demigods of the mundane worlds
The four Kumaras
Hundreds of thousands of lakṣmīs or gopīs
The residents of Svetadvipa
questions.correct_answer: Hundreds of thousands of lakṣmīs or gopīs
questions.explanation: The scripture states that Govinda is always served with great reverence and affection by hundreds of thousands of lakṣmīs or gopīs.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary quality attributed to the primeval Lord Govinda regarding the desires of His devotees?
options: He is the one who grants liberation from material existence
He is the one who yielding all desire
He is the one who sustains the life of the cosmic creation
He is the one who destroys all obstacles
questions.correct_answer: He is the one who yielding all desire
questions.explanation: The verse describes Govinda as the primeval Lord who is yielding all desire, indicating His role as the fulfiller of all aspirations for those in His abode.

30.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is adept in playing on His flute, with blooming eyes like lotus petals with head decked with peacock's feather, with the figure of beauty tinged with the hue of blue clouds, and His unique loveliness charming millions of Cupids.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific visual characteristic is used to describe the blooming eyes of Lord Govinda?
options: Like the rising sun
Like lotus petals
Like the morning dew
Like polished emeralds
questions.correct_answer: Like lotus petals
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the Lord's eyes as blooming like lotus petals.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what adorns the head of the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: A golden crown
A peacock's feather
A garland of forest flowers
A crown of sandalwood paste
questions.correct_answer: A peacock's feather
questions.explanation: The text describes Govinda's head as being decked with a peacock's feather.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the unique color or hue of Govinda's beautiful figure?
options: The glow of molten gold
The hue of blue clouds
The brilliance of a lightning flash
The dark luster of a sapphire
questions.correct_answer: The hue of blue clouds
questions.explanation: The verse depicts the Lord's figure as being tinged with the hue of blue clouds.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which activity is Govinda described as being adept in?
options: Managing the material universes
Playing on His flute
Protecting the Vedas
Dancing with the celestial dancers
questions.correct_answer: Playing on His flute
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Govinda as the primeval Lord who is adept in playing on His flute.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the loveliness of Lord Govinda compared to the allure of worldly beings?
options: It surpasses the beauty of millions of Cupids
It equals the charm of the Gandharvas
It is beyond the reach of material comparison
It outshines the radiance of the demigods
questions.correct_answer: It surpasses the beauty of millions of Cupids
questions.explanation: The verse states that His unique loveliness is charming to millions of Cupids.

33.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is inaccessible to the Vedas, but obtainable by pure unalloyed devotion of the soul, who is without a second, who is not subject to decay, is without a beginning, whose form is endless, who is the beginning, and the eternal puruṣa; yet He is a person possessing the beauty of blooming youth.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific method by which the primeval Lord Govinda, who is inaccessible to the Vedas, can be obtained?
options: Through the performance of rigorous Vedic sacrifices
By the deep meditation of yogis in seclusion
By pure unalloyed devotion of the soul
Through the intellectual process of eliminating the mundane
questions.correct_answer: By pure unalloyed devotion of the soul
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Govinda is inaccessible to the Vedas but is obtainable by the pure unalloyed devotion of the soul.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the nature of Govinda's eternal form described regarding his age and appearance?
options: As an ancient sage possessing the wisdom of ages
As a person possessing the beauty of blooming youth
As a cosmic force without any personal identity
As a transcendental child who never grows
questions.correct_answer: As a person possessing the beauty of blooming youth
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by describing Govinda as a person possessing the beauty of blooming youth, despite being the eternal primeval Lord.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what can be said about the beginning of the primeval Lord, Govinda?
options: He has a beginning but no end
He is without a beginning
He appeared at the start of the creation of the material world
He emerged from the causal ocean as the first manifestation
questions.correct_answer: He is without a beginning
questions.explanation: The verse clearly defines the primeval Lord Govinda as one who is without a beginning and the eternal puruṣa.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what describes the uniqueness of Govinda's existence among all beings?
options: He is the greatest among many equal gods
He is the beginning and the end of all demigods
He is without a second
He is the first among the three forms of the Trinity
questions.correct_answer: He is without a second
questions.explanation: The text states that Govinda is 'without a second', emphasizing His unique, non-dual supremacy.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the duration and state of Govinda's transcendental form characterized?
options: It is subject to the changes of time
It is not subject to decay
It undergoes seasonal transformations
It is limited to the spiritual realm of Vaikuntha
questions.correct_answer: It is not subject to decay
questions.explanation: The text characterizes Govinda as not subject to decay and possessing a form that is endless.

34.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, only the tip of the toe of whose lotus feet is approached by the yogīs who aspire after the transcendental and betake themselves to prāṇāyāma by drilling the respiration; or by the jñānīs who try to find out the nondifferentiated Brahman by the process of elimination of the mundane, extending over thousands of millions of years.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how much of the lotus feet of Govinda are the yogīs able to approach through their rigorous practice of prāṇāyāma?
options: The entirety of His lotus feet
Only the tip of the toe
The ankle of His lotus feet
The sole of His lotus feet
questions.correct_answer: Only the tip of the toe
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that even after the strenuous effort of drilling the respiration, the yogis can only reach the tip of the toe of Govinda's lotus feet.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific method do the yogīs employ in their endeavor to reach the lotus feet of the primeval Lord?
options: Chanting the holy name
Drilling the respiration
Studying the Vedas
Offering opulent worship
questions.correct_answer: Drilling the respiration
questions.explanation: The text describes the yogis as those who betake themselves to prāṇāyāma by drilling the respiration in their attempt to reach Govinda.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate goal of the jñānīs who engage in the process of eliminating the mundane?
options: The personal form of Govinda
The nondifferentiated Brahman
The creation of the material world
The transcendental abode of Vaikuntha
questions.correct_answer: The nondifferentiated Brahman
questions.explanation: The verse states that the jñānīs try to find out the nondifferentiated Brahman by the process of elimination of the mundane.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the duration of time over which the jñānīs attempt to find the nondifferentiated Brahman?
options: Millions of years
Thousands of millions of years
Infinite cycles of creation
A single day of Brahma
questions.correct_answer: Thousands of millions of years
questions.explanation: The text notes that the jñānīs engage in their process of elimination extending over thousands of millions of years.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the effort of the yogīs compare to their access to the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: They attain full vision by their effort
They only reach the tip of the toe of His lotus feet
They are entirely rejected by the Lord
They become one with the Lord's form
questions.correct_answer: They only reach the tip of the toe of His lotus feet
questions.explanation: The verse highlights the extreme difficulty and limited result of the yogic path in accessing the primeval Lord Govinda.

35.verse_text: He is an undifferentiated entity as there is no distinction between potency and the possessor thereof. In His work of creation of millions of worlds, His potency remains inseparable. All the universes exist in Him and He is present in His fullness in every one of the atoms that are scattered throughout the universe, at one and the same time. Such is the primeval Lord whom I adore.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the relationship between the Supreme Lord and His potency manifest during the creation of millions of worlds?
options: The potency acts independently from the Lord
The potency remains inseparable from the Lord
The potency is exhausted after the creation of each universe
The potency only exists within the spiritual realm of Goloka
questions.correct_answer: The potency remains inseparable from the Lord
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that in His work of creation, the Lord's potency remains inseparable from Him as there is no distinction between the potency and the possessor thereof.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the extent of Govinda’s presence within the material creation?
options: He resides only in the central heart of each universe
He manifests only when invoked by the demigods
He is present in His fullness in every atom of the universe
He remains entirely aloof from the atomic structure of the cosmos
questions.correct_answer: He is present in His fullness in every atom of the universe
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the Lord is present in His fullness in every one of the atoms that are scattered throughout the universe at one and the same time.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the Supreme Lord regarding His own identity and potency?
options: He is a differentiated entity who separates Himself from His potencies
He is an undifferentiated entity where no distinction exists between Him and His potency
He assumes the form of His potency to interact with the material world
He remains distinct from His potency until the final dissolution
questions.correct_answer: He is an undifferentiated entity where no distinction exists between Him and His potency
questions.explanation: The verse begins by defining Him as an undifferentiated entity because there is no distinction between the potency and the possessor thereof.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the scope of the universes that exist within Govinda?
options: Only the specific universe created for the devotees
All the countless universes
Only the spiritual realm of Vaikuntha
A limited number of universes assigned to the primary demigods
questions.correct_answer: All the countless universes
questions.explanation: The text confirms that all the universes exist in Him, referencing the infinite scope of His creative potency.

36.verse_text: I adore the same Govinda, the primeval Lord, in whose praise men, who are imbued with devotion, sing the mantra-sūktas told by the Vedas, by gaining their appropriate beauty, greatness, thrones, conveyances and ornaments.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what do devotees obtain when they sing the mantra-sūktas in praise of the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: Material wealth, fame, and temporal kingdoms
Appropriate beauty, greatness, thrones, conveyances, and ornaments
The ability to control the movements of the planets
Direct vision of the cosmic form of the universe
questions.correct_answer: Appropriate beauty, greatness, thrones, conveyances, and ornaments
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that those imbued with devotion gain these specific spiritual opulences and attributes by singing the praises of Govinda.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what kind of individuals are qualified to sing the mantra-sūktas of the Vedas in praise of Govinda?
options: Those who have mastered the six systems of Indian philosophy
Those who are born in the highest priestly families
Men who are deeply imbued with devotion
Those who have practiced extreme asceticism in the forest
questions.correct_answer: Men who are deeply imbued with devotion
questions.explanation: The verse highlights that it is specifically those men who are imbued with devotion who are engaged in singing the mantra-sūktas to glorify the primeval Lord.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary object of the mantra-sūktas found within the Vedas?
options: The various demigods presiding over the elements
The impersonal Brahman effulgence
The primeval Lord Govinda
The expansion of Maha-Visnu in the Causal Ocean
questions.correct_answer: The primeval Lord Govinda
questions.explanation: The verse clearly identifies that the mantra-sūktas told by the Vedas are sung in praise of the primeval Lord, Govinda.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what happens to the practitioners who engage in singing the mantra-sūktas as an act of adoration?
options: They are liberated from the cycle of birth and death forever
They attain a spiritual stature characterized by beauty and greatness
They are granted the position of demigods in the celestial hierarchy
They receive the power to create universes like Brahma
questions.correct_answer: They attain a spiritual stature characterized by beauty and greatness
questions.explanation: The text confirms that by singing these praises, the devotees gain their appropriate beauty and greatness, reflecting the grace of the Lord.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how do the mantra-sūktas of the Vedas function in relation to the primeval Lord?
options: They act as tools for cosmic dissolution
They serve to manifest the secondary creation
They are utilized by devotees to sing the praises of Govinda
They function as the material blueprints for the planets
questions.correct_answer: They are utilized by devotees to sing the praises of Govinda
questions.explanation: The verse describes the mantra-sūktas told by the Vedas as being sung by devotees to adore the primeval Lord Govinda.

37.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, residing in His own realm, Goloka, with Rādhā, resembling His own spiritual figure, the embodiment of the ecstatic potency possessed of the sixty-four artistic activities, in the company of Her confidantes [sakhīs], embodiments of the extensions of Her bodily form, permeated and vitalized by His ever-blissful spiritual rasa.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific number of artistic activities is attributed to the ecstatic potency, Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, who accompanies Govinda in Goloka?
options: Sixteen
Thirty-two
Sixty-four
One hundred and eight
questions.correct_answer: Sixty-four
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes the ecstatic potency, Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, as being possessed of the sixty-four artistic activities.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the relationship between the confidantes (sakhīs) and Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī described in their eternal service to Govinda?
options: As independent celestial deities
As embodiments of the extensions of Her bodily form
As manifestations of the material energy
As souls who have attained liberation through austerity
questions.correct_answer: As embodiments of the extensions of Her bodily form
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the sakhīs are not separate entities in essence but are embodiments of the extensions of Rādhārāṇī's own bodily form.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the realm where Govinda resides eternally with His beloved Rādhārāṇī?
options: Svetadvipa
Vaikuntha
Goloka
Mathura
questions.correct_answer: Goloka
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names Goloka as the primeval Lord Govinda's own realm where He resides with Rādhā.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the spiritual figure of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī characterized in relation to Govinda?
options: As a goddess of the material world
As an independent queen of the Vaikuntha planets
As resembling His own spiritual figure
As the creator of the causal ocean
questions.correct_answer: As resembling His own spiritual figure
questions.explanation: The text describes Rādhā as resembling Govinda's own spiritual figure, highlighting their eternal, transcendental union.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what force is said to permeate and vitalize the company of sakhīs who serve Govinda and Rādhārāṇī?
options: The creative power of Brahma
The ever-blissful spiritual rasa
The illusory energy of Maya
The gravity of the material universes
questions.correct_answer: The ever-blissful spiritual rasa
questions.explanation: The verse states that the company of sakhīs is permeated and vitalized by Govinda's ever-blissful spiritual rasa.

38.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is Śyāmasundara, Kṛṣṇa Himself with inconceivable innumerable attributes, whom the pure devotees see in their heart of hearts with the eye of devotion tinged with the salve of love.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the divine name of the primeval Lord who is described as Śyāmasundara and the possessor of inconceivable attributes?
options: Nārāyaṇa
Govinda
Vāsudeva
Kṣīrodakaśāyī
questions.correct_answer: Govinda
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states, 'I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is Śyāmasundara, Kṛṣṇa Himself...'

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, by what specific tool or instrument do pure devotees perceive the form of Śyāmasundara within their hearts?
options: The eye of rigorous logic
The eye of vedic scholarship
The eye of devotion tinged with the salve of love
The eye of meditation on the void
questions.correct_answer: The eye of devotion tinged with the salve of love
questions.explanation: The text explains that pure devotees see the Lord in their heart of hearts with the eye of devotion tinged with the salve of love.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, where do the pure devotees behold the transcendental form of the Lord?
options: In the external material universe
In their heart of hearts
In the highest sphere of the impersonal Brahman
In the assembly of the demigods
questions.correct_answer: In their heart of hearts
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the Lord is seen by pure devotees in their heart of hearts.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what descriptive title or name is given to the primeval Lord, Govinda, regarding His personal beauty?
options: Brahmānanda
Śyāmasundara
Ananta-śeṣa
Yogeśvara
questions.correct_answer: Śyāmasundara
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Govinda, the primeval Lord, as Śyāmasundara.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the attributes possessed by the primeval Lord, Kṛṣṇa?
options: Materially categorized
Inconceivable and innumerable
Limited by time and space
Subject to the modes of nature
questions.correct_answer: Inconceivable and innumerable
questions.explanation: The text describes Kṛṣṇa as having inconceivable innumerable attributes.

39.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who manifested Himself personally as Kṛṣṇa and the different avatāras in the world in the forms of Rāma, Nṛsiṁha, Vāmana, etc., as His subjective portions.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the primeval Lord Govinda manifest Himself in the mundane world in relation to His various incarnations?
options: As His own subjective portions
As separate independent deities
As reflections of the material energy
As temporary manifestations of illusion
questions.correct_answer: As His own subjective portions
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Govinda manifests as Rāma, Nṛsiṁha, Vāmana, etc., as His own subjective portions (svāṁśa).

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which of the following is specifically mentioned as a subjective portion of the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: Lord Brahmā
Lord Nṛsiṁha
Lord Śiva
Lord Gaṇeśa
questions.correct_answer: Lord Nṛsiṁha
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Rāma, Nṛsiṁha, and Vāmana as examples of the subjective portions through which Govinda manifests.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary identity of the Lord who appears as the various avatāras such as Vāmana and Rāma?
options: Kṛṣṇa
Nārāyaṇa
Mahā-Viṣṇu
Kṣīrodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The text states that Govinda manifests Himself personally as Kṛṣṇa and then manifests the different avatāras as His subjective portions.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, when Govinda manifests as Rāma, Nṛsiṁha, and Vāmana, what is the theological status of these forms?
options: They are mere creations of māyā
They are His subjective portions
They are entirely separate from Him
They are expansions of the material soul
questions.correct_answer: They are His subjective portions
questions.explanation: The scripture confirms that these avatāras are His subjective portions (svāṁśa) through which He manifests in the world.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the relationship between Kṛṣṇa and the various avatāras defined?
options: Kṛṣṇa is the source and the avatāras are His subjective portions
The avatāras are the cause of Kṛṣṇa's appearance
Kṛṣṇa is one of many avatāras of the supreme Lord
The avatāras are independent lords of the material realm
questions.correct_answer: Kṛṣṇa is the source and the avatāras are His subjective portions
questions.explanation: The verse establishes that Govinda manifests personally as Kṛṣṇa, and the other incarnations appear as subjective portions of that same Lord.

40.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, whose effulgence is the source of the nondifferentiated Brahman mentioned in the Upaniṣads, being differentiated from the infinity of glories of the mundane universe appears as the indivisible, infinite, limitless, truth.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate source of the nondifferentiated Brahman described in the Upaniṣads?
options: The effulgence of Govinda
The expansion of Maha-Visnu
The radiance of the Garbhodakasayi Lord
The manifestation of the internal potency
questions.correct_answer: The effulgence of Govinda
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Govinda's effulgence is the source of the nondifferentiated Brahman.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the transcendental truth of the Lord appear when differentiated from the glories of the mundane universe?
options: As a flickering light in the heart
As the indivisible, infinite, limitless truth
As a combination of three divine qualities
As the creator of the material elements
questions.correct_answer: As the indivisible, infinite, limitless truth
questions.explanation: The verse describes that when the Lord is differentiated from mundane glories, He appears as the indivisible, infinite, and limitless truth.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is Govinda described as being in relation to the nondifferentiated Brahman?
options: The primeval Lord whose effulgence constitutes Brahman
The silent witness within the Brahman
A subordinate aspect of the Brahman effulgence
The protector of the Brahman effulgence
questions.correct_answer: The primeval Lord whose effulgence constitutes Brahman
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that Govinda is the primeval Lord and the source of the effulgence known as Brahman.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the truth that exists beyond the infinite glories of the mundane world?
options: It is fragmented and localized
It is the source of all material creation
It is indivisible, infinite, and limitless
It is subject to the influence of time
questions.correct_answer: It is indivisible, infinite, and limitless
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the truth beyond the mundane universe is characterized as indivisible, infinite, and limitless.

questions.question: Based on the teachings of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the connection between the Brahman mentioned in the Upaniṣads and the Lord of Goloka?
options: Brahman is the shadow of Govinda's effulgence
Govinda's effulgence is the source of Brahman
Brahman is the physical body of Govinda
Govinda is a manifestation within Brahman
questions.correct_answer: Govinda's effulgence is the source of Brahman
questions.explanation: The verse directly identifies Govinda's effulgence as the origin of the nondifferentiated Brahman mentioned in the Vedic scriptures.

41.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is the absolute substantive principle being the ultimate entity in the form of the support of all existence whose external potency embodies the threefold mundane qualities, viz., sattva, rajas, and tamas and diffuses the Vedic knowledge regarding the mundane world.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate nature of the primeval Lord Govinda in relation to the totality of existence?
options: The absolute substantive principle and support of all existence
The creator of the material elements of earth, water, and fire
The eternal observer of the conditioned souls in the ocean of birth
The master of the cosmic dance who maintains the planetary orbits
questions.correct_answer: The absolute substantive principle and support of all existence
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Govinda as the absolute substantive principle and the ultimate entity that serves as the support of all existence.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific role does the external potency of Govinda play within the mundane realm?
options: It manifests the personal forms of the demigods for worship
It embodies the threefold mundane qualities and diffuses Vedic knowledge
It obscures the vision of the conditioned souls to prevent liberation
It creates the spiritual abodes within the heart of the yogi
questions.correct_answer: It embodies the threefold mundane qualities and diffuses Vedic knowledge
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Govinda's external potency embodies the three modes of material nature (sattva, rajas, and tamas) and spreads knowledge of the Vedas concerning the mundane world.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what are the threefold mundane qualities mentioned in association with the external potency of Govinda?
options: Sattva, rajas, and tamas
Sat, cit, and ananda
Yoga-maya, maha-maya, and jiva-maya
Karma, jnana, and vairagya
questions.correct_answer: Sattva, rajas, and tamas
questions.explanation: The text identifies the external potency as embodying the three mundane qualities of goodness (sattva), passion (rajas), and ignorance (tamas).

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary purpose of the diffusion of Vedic knowledge facilitated by the external potency of the Lord?
options: To regulate the affairs of the mundane world
To provide liberation to the inhabitants of the heavenly planets
To confuse the souls bound by the cycle of reincarnation
To reveal the hidden secrets of the spiritual sky
questions.correct_answer: To regulate the affairs of the mundane world
questions.explanation: The text states that the external potency diffuses Vedic knowledge specifically regarding the mundane world to govern its functions.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the status of Govinda defined in relation to His role as the ultimate entity?
options: He is the primeval Lord and the absolute substantive principle
He is the secondary cause of the cosmic manifestation
He is the protector of the devotees who live in the material world
He is the manifestation of the Lord in the form of time
questions.correct_answer: He is the primeval Lord and the absolute substantive principle
questions.explanation: The verse opens by addressing Govinda as the primeval Lord and describing Him as the absolute substantive principle.

42.verse_text: I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, whose glory ever triumphantly dominates the mundane world by the activity of His own pastimes, being reflected in the mind of recollecting souls as the transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the glory of Govinda manifest within the consciousness of those who are recollecting Him?
options: As the transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa
As the source of all material manifestations and Vedic knowledge
As the supreme controller of the threefold mundane qualities
As the origin of all avataras and cosmic expansion
questions.correct_answer: As the transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Govinda's glory is reflected in the mind of recollecting souls as the transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what does the glory of the primeval Lord Govinda triumphantly dominate?
options: The various abodes of the demigods
The mundane world
The causal ocean
The effulgence of Brahman
questions.correct_answer: The mundane world
questions.explanation: The verse declares that Govinda's glory ever triumphantly dominates the mundane world through the activity of His own pastimes.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, by what specific means does Govinda's glory triumphantly dominate the mundane world?
options: By the activity of His own pastimes
By the expansion of His external potency
By the imposition of the threefold mundane qualities
By the diffusion of transcendental knowledge
questions.correct_answer: By the activity of His own pastimes
questions.explanation: The text confirms that His glory dominates the mundane world specifically through the activity of His own pastimes.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the entity that recollecting souls perceive as Govinda?
options: A form of pure material sattva
A transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa
A manifestation of the impersonal light of Brahman
A reflection of the external potency of the Lord
questions.correct_answer: A transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa
questions.explanation: The text clearly defines the Lord as appearing to the recollecting soul as an entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary prerequisite for experiencing the glory of Govinda in the mind?
options: The performance of severe penances
The study of the Vedas
The process of recollecting the Lord
The realization of the threefold mundane qualities
questions.correct_answer: The process of recollecting the Lord
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that this glory is reflected in the mind of 'recollecting souls' (those who remember the Lord).

43.verse_text: Lowest of all is located Devī-dhāma [mundane world], next above it is Maheśa-dhāma [abode of Maheśa]; above Maheśa-dhāma is placed Hari-dhāma [abode of Hari] and above them all is located Kṛṣṇa's own realm named Goloka. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, who has allotted their respective authorities to the rulers of those graded realms.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the hierarchical arrangement of the various realms starting from the lowest?
options: Devī-dhāma, Maheśa-dhāma, Hari-dhāma, and Goloka
Maheśa-dhāma, Devī-dhāma, Hari-dhāma, and Goloka
Goloka, Hari-dhāma, Maheśa-dhāma, and Devī-dhāma
Hari-dhāma, Devī-dhāma, Maheśa-dhāma, and Goloka
questions.correct_answer: Devī-dhāma, Maheśa-dhāma, Hari-dhāma, and Goloka
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists the progression of the realms starting from the lowest mundane world, Devī-dhāma, up to the supreme abode of Goloka.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who is identified as the one who allots the respective authorities to the rulers of the graded realms?
options: Lord Brahmā
Lord Śiva
Lord Govinda
Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: Lord Govinda
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by stating that it is the primeval Lord Govinda who grants the specific authority to the rulers of these graded realms.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific name given to the mundane world within the hierarchy of realms?
options: Maheśa-dhāma
Devī-dhāma
Hari-dhāma
Goloka
questions.correct_answer: Devī-dhāma
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the lowest of all realms, the mundane world, as Devī-dhāma.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which realm is described as being situated above Maheśa-dhāma?
options: Devī-dhāma
Goloka
Hari-dhāma
Vaikuṇṭha-loka
questions.correct_answer: Hari-dhāma
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Hari-dhāma is placed directly above Maheśa-dhāma.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which realm stands at the very summit, above all other graded realms?
options: Hari-dhāma
Maheśa-dhāma
Goloka
Svetadvīpa
questions.correct_answer: Goloka
questions.explanation: The text states that above all other mentioned realms, Kṛṣṇa's own realm named Goloka is located.

44.verse_text: The external potency Māyā who is of the nature of the shadow of the cit potency, is worshiped by all people as Durgā, the creating, preserving and destroying agency of this mundane world. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda in accordance with whose will Durgā conducts herself.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the external potency, Durgā, characterized in relation to the supreme cit potency?
options: As the direct manifestation of the cit potency
As the shadow of the cit potency
As the expanded form of the cit potency
As the obscured reflection of the cit potency
questions.correct_answer: As the shadow of the cit potency
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the external potency Māyā, who is worshiped as Durgā, is the nature of the shadow of the cit potency.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific roles are attributed to Durgā within the governance of this mundane world?
options: Creating, preserving, and destroying
Manifesting, protecting, and annihilating
Expanding, nurturing, and dissolving
Formulating, maintaining, and liberating
questions.correct_answer: Creating, preserving, and destroying
questions.explanation: The verse defines Durgā as the creating, preserving, and destroying agency of this mundane world.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, whose will governs the activities and conduct of Durgā?
options: Lord Śambhu
Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu
Lord Govinda
The Supreme Puruṣa
questions.correct_answer: Lord Govinda
questions.explanation: The text confirms the adoration of Lord Govinda, in accordance with whose will Durgā conducts herself.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the relationship between the mundane agency Durgā and the cit potency?
options: They are identical in function
Durgā is a shadow of the cit potency
The cit potency is a manifestation of Durgā
They exist as independent parallel powers
questions.correct_answer: Durgā is a shadow of the cit potency
questions.explanation: The scripture clearly identifies the external potency, known as Durgā, as being the shadow of the cit potency.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the worship of the agency known as Durgā described regarding the people of this world?
options: She is worshiped by all people
She is worshiped by the devotees only
She is worshipped exclusively by the demigods
She is worshipped secretly by the sages
questions.correct_answer: She is worshiped by all people
questions.explanation: The verse states that the external potency, Māyā, is worshiped by all people as Durgā.

45.verse_text: Just as milk is transformed into curd by the action of acids, but yet the effect curd is neither same as, nor different from, its cause, viz., milk, so I adore the primeval Lord Govinda of whom the state of Śambhu is a transformation for the performance of the work of destruction.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific transformation is utilized as a metaphor to explain the emergence of Śambhu from the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: The conversion of milk into curd through the action of acids
The manifestation of fire from the friction of wooden sticks
The transformation of water into vapor by the heat of the sun
The crystallization of liquid sap into solid resin
questions.correct_answer: The conversion of milk into curd through the action of acids
questions.explanation: The text explicitly uses the analogy of milk turning into curd via acids to describe how Śambhu is a transformation of Govinda for the purpose of destruction.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary function for which Lord Govinda manifests the state of Śambhu?
options: The creation of the material universes
The performance of the work of destruction
The maintenance of the cosmic order
The delivery of the Vedas to the living entities
questions.correct_answer: The performance of the work of destruction
questions.explanation: The verse clearly states that Śambhu is a transformation of Govinda specifically for the performance of the work of destruction.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what philosophical relationship exists between the curd and its cause, milk, when discussing the nature of Śambhu?
options: They are completely identical in every manifest form
They are entirely unrelated and separate entities
The effect is neither the same as nor different from the cause
The cause ceases to exist once the effect is manifest
questions.correct_answer: The effect is neither the same as nor different from the cause
questions.explanation: The scripture employs the logic of acintya-bhedabheda-tattva, noting that the curd is neither the same as nor different from the milk.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what substance is identified as the cause which, through the action of acids, brings about the state of Śambhu?
options: Nectar
Ghee
Milk
Honey
questions.correct_answer: Milk
questions.explanation: The verse compares the transformation of the Lord into Śambhu to the process of milk being transformed into curd.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, the transformation of Lord Govinda into Śambhu is compared to the action of what agent upon milk?
options: Heat
Acids
Time
Churning
questions.correct_answer: Acids
questions.explanation: The text identifies the action of acids as the specific catalyst that transforms milk into the curd-like manifestation of Śambhu.

46.verse_text: The light of one candle being communicated to other candles, although it burns separately in them, is the same in its quality. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda who exhibits Himself equally in the same mobile manner in His various manifestations.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the relationship between the original flame and the candles it ignites used to describe the nature of Govinda's manifestations?
options: They are qualitatively identical despite burning as separate flames
They are reflections that cease to exist when the original candle is extinguished
They are fundamentally different in potency despite sharing the same light
They are merely temporary appearances that lack the substance of the original
questions.correct_answer: They are qualitatively identical despite burning as separate flames
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly uses the analogy of one candle lighting others to explain that while Govinda exhibits Himself in various manifestations, they all remain identical in quality.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, in what manner does the primeval Lord Govinda exhibit Himself across His various transcendental forms?
options: He exhibits Himself equally in the same mobile manner
He exhibits Himself through the gradual evolution of material creation
He exhibits Himself only within the heart of the surrendered soul
He exhibits Himself as a static light devoid of personal form
questions.correct_answer: He exhibits Himself equally in the same mobile manner
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the Lord maintains His identity and quality equally across all His various manifestations, acting in the same mobile way.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what does the example of the candle's light signify regarding the Supreme Personality of Godhead?
options: The oneness and equality of the quality of the Supreme Lord in all His expansions
The necessity of material intermediaries to perceive the spiritual light
The fading of potency as the original light expands into many forms
The distinction between the Lord's physical form and His internal radiance
questions.correct_answer: The oneness and equality of the quality of the Supreme Lord in all His expansions
questions.explanation: The verse uses the candle analogy to illustrate that despite multiple manifestations, the quality of the Lord remains one and the same.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how are the various manifestations of Govinda described in comparison to the primary source?
options: They are qualitatively one with the Lord
They are subordinate servants with limited potencies
They are material representations of His spiritual form
They are different entities acting under His direction
questions.correct_answer: They are qualitatively one with the Lord
questions.explanation: The verse emphasizes that the manifestations share the same quality as the original, just as the light of one candle is the same in all others.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate conclusion concerning the nature of Govinda's appearances in the spiritual and material realms?
options: The Lord remains non-different in quality across all His forms
The Lord diminishes in power the further He moves from the center of Goloka
The Lord adopts material qualities to interact with the conditioned souls
The Lord hides His true identity within His various manifestations
questions.correct_answer: The Lord remains non-different in quality across all His forms
questions.explanation: The scripture confirms the consistency of Govinda's transcendental quality in all His manifestations, likened to the identical quality of light from multiple candles.

47.verse_text: I adore the primeval Lord Govinda who assuming His own great subjective form, who bears the name of Śeṣa, replete with the all-accommodating potency, and reposing in the Causal Ocean with the infinity of the world in the pores of His hair, enjoys creative sleep [yoga-nidrā].

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific name of the great subjective form assumed by Lord Govinda while He reposes within the Causal Ocean?
options: Śeṣa
Kṣīrodakaśāyī
Aniruddha
Gar-bhodakaśāyī
questions.correct_answer: Śeṣa
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Lord Govinda assumes His own great subjective form, which bears the name of Śeṣa, to repose in the Causal Ocean.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the name of the transcendental state of creative sleep enjoyed by the primeval Lord while reposing in the Causal Ocean?
options: Yoga-nidrā
Svapa-samadhi
Caitanya-lila
Mahā-pralaya
questions.correct_answer: Yoga-nidrā
questions.explanation: The text describes that while reposing in the Causal Ocean, the Lord enjoys creative sleep, known as yoga-nidrā.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, where does the infinity of the mundane worlds reside while Lord Govinda reposes in His subjective form of Śeṣa?
options: Within the pores of His hair
Upon the hoods of Ananta-Sesa
Inside the lotus of His navel
Within the effulgence of the Brahman
questions.correct_answer: Within the pores of His hair
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the infinity of the world is contained within the pores of the hair of the Lord's subjective form, Śeṣa.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which specific potency is the subjective form of the Lord, Śeṣa, described as being replete with?
options: The all-accommodating potency
The potency of destruction
The potency of material illusion
The potency of time
questions.correct_answer: The all-accommodating potency
questions.explanation: The text describes the form of Śeṣa as being replete with the all-accommodating potency while He reposes in the Causal Ocean.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what activity is the Lord engaged in while He rests in the Causal Ocean in His form as Śeṣa?
options: Enjoying creative sleep
Supervising the movement of the stars
Listening to the Vedas from the sages
Meditating on the liberation of souls
questions.correct_answer: Enjoying creative sleep
questions.explanation: The verse states that the Lord, in His form as Śeṣa, reposes in the Causal Ocean and enjoys creative sleep (yoga-nidrā).

48.verse_text: Brahmā and other lords of the mundane worlds, appearing from the pores of hair of Mahā-Viṣṇu, remain alive as long as the duration of one exhalation of the latter [Mahā-Viṣṇu]. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda of whose subjective personality Mahā-Viṣṇu is the portion of portion.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how long do Brahmā and the other presiding lords of the mundane worlds exist in relation to the transcendental body of Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: For the duration of one inhalation of Mahā-Viṣṇu
For the duration of one exhalation of Mahā-Viṣṇu
For the duration of one blink of Mahā-Viṣṇu
For the duration of one dream of Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: For the duration of one exhalation of Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Brahmā and other lords of mundane worlds remain alive only as long as the duration of one exhalation of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the exact ontological relationship between the primeval Lord Govinda and Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: Mahā-Viṣṇu is the direct expansion of Govinda
Mahā-Viṣṇu is a plenary portion of Govinda
Mahā-Viṣṇu is the portion of a portion of Govinda
Mahā-Viṣṇu is the shadow potency of Govinda
questions.correct_answer: Mahā-Viṣṇu is the portion of a portion of Govinda
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that Mahā-Viṣṇu, who oversees the mundane worlds, is only a portion of a portion of the primeval Lord Govinda.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, where do the various Brahmās and lords of the mundane worlds originate from before they begin their creative duties?
options: From the lotus emerging from the navel of Mahā-Viṣṇu
From the pores of the hair of Mahā-Viṣṇu
From the effulgence radiating from the spiritual sky
From the Causal Ocean where Mahā-Viṣṇu reclines
questions.correct_answer: From the pores of the hair of Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The verse specifically mentions that Brahmā and other lords of the mundane worlds appear from the pores of the hair of Mahā-Viṣṇu.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the specific role of the primeval Lord Govinda regarding the existence of Mahā-Viṣṇu?
options: He is the source of Mahā-Viṣṇu's divine rest
He is the object of adoration for Mahā-Viṣṇu
He is the progenitor from whom Mahā-Viṣṇu emanates
He is the witness to Mahā-Viṣṇu's creative sleep
questions.correct_answer: He is the progenitor from whom Mahā-Viṣṇu emanates
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by emphasizing the adoration of Govinda, as even Mahā-Viṣṇu is merely a portion of His supreme personality.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which entity is described as reposing in the Causal Ocean while possessing the infinity of the world within His pores?
options: Lord Śeṣa
Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu
Lord Garbhodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
Lord Kṣīrodakaśāyī Viṣṇu
questions.correct_answer: Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The context provided confirms that Mahā-Viṣṇu is the one who reposes in the Causal Ocean and from whose hair pores the mundane worlds manifest.

49.verse_text: I adore the primeval Lord Govinda from whom the separated subjective portion Brahmā receives his power for the regulation of the mundane world, just as the sun manifests some portion of his own light in all the effulgent gems that bear the names of sūryakānta, etc.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the primeval Lord Govinda bestow the potency for universal regulation upon Brahmā?
options: By directly bestowing the Vedas through his transcendental voice
By manifesting a portion of his power similar to the sun illuminating the suryakanta gem
By placing his own divine effulgence into the heart of the creator
By descending into the mundane world to personally instruct the demigods
questions.correct_answer: By manifesting a portion of his power similar to the sun illuminating the suryakanta gem
questions.explanation: The text explains that Govinda empowers Brahma in a manner analogous to how the sun manifests its light within effulgent gems like the suryakanta.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific type of subjective portion is Lord Brahmā described as in relation to the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: A direct expansion of the Supreme Personality of Godhead
A separate subjective portion
The complete embodiment of the Lord's creative energy
The primordial soul of the material universe
questions.correct_answer: A separate subjective portion
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly characterizes Brahmā as the separated subjective portion who derives his regulatory power from Govinda.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the primary function for which Brahmā receives power from Lord Govinda?
options: The destruction of all mundane universes at the end of time
The maintenance of the spiritual abodes in Vaikuntha
The regulation of the mundane world
The expansion of the material elements into infinite space
questions.correct_answer: The regulation of the mundane world
questions.explanation: The text states that Brahmā receives power from Govinda specifically for the purpose of regulating the mundane world.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which specific gem is mentioned as an example of light manifestation in relation to Govinda’s empowerment of Brahmā?
options: Cintamani
Suryakanta
Kaustubha
Vaidurya
questions.correct_answer: Suryakanta
questions.explanation: The text uses the suryakanta gem as the specific illustration of how Govinda's power is reflected through his separated portions.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does the sun relate to the effulgent gems described in the context of Govinda’s power?
options: The sun creates the gems through its intense heat
The sun manifests its light within the gems
The sun hides its brilliance behind the gems
The sun is worshipped by the gems as the supreme regulator
questions.correct_answer: The sun manifests its light within the gems
questions.explanation: The text explains that just as the sun manifests a portion of its light in effulgent gems, so too does Govinda empower Brahmā.

50.verse_text: I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, whose lotus feet are always held by Gaṇeśa upon the pair of tumuli protruding from his elephant head in order to obtain power for his function of destroying all the obstacles on the path of progress of the three worlds.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific purpose does Gaṇeśa achieve by holding the lotus feet of Lord Govinda upon the tumuli of his elephant head?
options: To acquire the power to destroy all obstacles within the three worlds
To obtain the intelligence required to create the material universe
To become liberated from the influence of the three modes of nature
To receive the blessing of eternal devotion to the Lord's pastimes
questions.correct_answer: To acquire the power to destroy all obstacles within the three worlds
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Gaṇeśa holds Govinda's lotus feet to obtain the power to destroy obstacles in the path of progress of the three worlds.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what physical feature of Gaṇeśa serves as the resting place for the lotus feet of Govinda?
options: The pair of tumuli protruding from his elephant head
The palms of his four hands
The crown of his head
His broad lotus-like chest
questions.correct_answer: The pair of tumuli protruding from his elephant head
questions.explanation: The text describes that Gaṇeśa holds Govinda's feet specifically upon the pair of tumuli protruding from his elephant head.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, who is the primeval Lord whose feet are worshipped by Gaṇeśa to attain the capacity to remove obstacles?
options: Govinda
Viṣṇu
Brahmā
Śiva
questions.correct_answer: Govinda
questions.explanation: The verse begins and ends with the adoration of the primeval Lord Govinda, whose feet Gaṇeśa holds.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the extent of the influence of the obstacles that Gaṇeśa is empowered to destroy?
options: The three worlds
The fourteen planetary systems
The material manifestation known as Devī-dhāma
The path leading to the spiritual sky
questions.correct_answer: The three worlds
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Gaṇeśa destroys obstacles specifically on the path of progress of the three worlds.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the relationship between Gaṇeśa's service and the authority he exercises?
options: He holds the lotus feet of Govinda to obtain the power necessary for his designated function
He is given the power directly by Śiva to manage the mundane worlds
He attains authority through his own penance without external assistance
He is empowered by Brahmā to regulate the activities of all living entities
questions.correct_answer: He holds the lotus feet of Govinda to obtain the power necessary for his designated function
questions.explanation: The scripture clarifies that Gaṇeśa's ability to destroy obstacles is a direct result of the power he obtains from holding the lotus feet of Govinda.

51.verse_text: The three worlds are composed of the nine elements, viz., fire, earth, ether, water, air, direction, time, soul and mind. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda from whom they originate, in whom they exist and into whom they enter at the time of the universal cataclysm.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how many elements are mentioned as the fundamental components of the three worlds?
options: Seven elements
Nine elements
Ten elements
Twelve elements
questions.correct_answer: Nine elements
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the three worlds are composed of nine elements: fire, earth, ether, water, air, direction, time, soul, and mind.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which of the following is explicitly listed as one of the nine elements that constitute the three worlds?
options: The ego (ahankara)
The mahat-tattva
The mind
The pradhana
questions.correct_answer: The mind
questions.explanation: The verse lists fire, earth, ether, water, air, direction, time, soul, and mind as the constituents of the three worlds.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the relationship between the three worlds and Lord Govinda regarding their ultimate existence?
options: They are eternal expansions of His internal potency
They originate from Him, exist in Him, and enter into Him at the time of cataclysm
They are independent entities sustained by His indirect rays
They are the material manifestations created by Maha-Visnu without Govinda's direct involvement
questions.correct_answer: They originate from Him, exist in Him, and enter into Him at the time of cataclysm
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that the three worlds originate from, exist in, and return to Govinda during the universal cataclysm.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which of the following is NOT included in the list of nine elements that compose the three worlds?
options: Ether
Direction
Soul
Intelligence
questions.correct_answer: Intelligence
questions.explanation: Intelligence is not listed among the nine elements in this specific verse, which identifies fire, earth, ether, water, air, direction, time, soul, and mind.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what happens to the nine elements of the three worlds at the time of the universal cataclysm?
options: They are destroyed by the wrath of Lord Siva
They are reabsorbed into the material pradhana
They enter into the primeval Lord Govinda
They are transformed into the spiritual effulgence of Brahman
questions.correct_answer: They enter into the primeval Lord Govinda
questions.explanation: The verse states that at the time of the universal cataclysm, the elements enter into the primeval Lord Govinda.

52.verse_text: The sun who is the king of all the planets, full of infinite effulgence, the image of the good soul, is as the eye of this world. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda in pursuance of whose order the sun performs his journey mounting the wheel of time.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the symbolic role of the sun in relation to the inhabitants of the material world?
options: The sun acts as the heart of the world
The sun acts as the eye of the world
The sun acts as the life-breath of the world
The sun acts as the king of the devas
questions.correct_answer: The sun acts as the eye of the world
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the sun, who is the king of all planets, serves as the eye of this world.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific metaphor describes the movement of the sun across the heavens?
options: A chariot drawn by seven horses
A steady orbit around the pole star
Mounting the wheel of time
A circular path guided by the Vedas
questions.correct_answer: Mounting the wheel of time
questions.explanation: The verse describes the sun performing his journey by mounting the wheel of time in obedience to the order of Govinda.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the ultimate source of the sun's authority and infinite effulgence?
options: The primeval Lord Govinda
The energy of the Causal Ocean
The brilliance of Maha-Visnu
The internal potency of Lord Brahma
questions.correct_answer: The primeval Lord Govinda
questions.explanation: The text states that the speaker adores the primeval Lord Govinda, in pursuance of whose order the sun functions.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how is the nature of the sun described beyond its radiance?
options: As the protector of the Vedas
As the image of the good soul
As the manifestation of the Lord's breath
As the origin of all living entities
questions.correct_answer: As the image of the good soul
questions.explanation: The verse specifically characterizes the sun as the image of the good soul, full of infinite effulgence.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what title is given to the sun in recognition of its position among all planetary bodies?
options: The regulator of the universes
The king of all the planets
The supreme light of the heavens
The traveler of the cosmic path
questions.correct_answer: The king of all the planets
questions.explanation: The verse introduces the sun with the title of being the king of all the planets.

53.verse_text: I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, by whose conferred power are maintained the manifested potencies, that are found to exist, of all virtues, all vices, the Vedas, the penances and all jīvas, from Brahmā to the meanest insect.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the extent of the range of living beings whose potencies are maintained by the conferred power of Lord Govinda?
options: From the demigods down to the humans
From the great sages down to the forest animals
From Brahmā to the meanest insect
From the residents of Vaikuṇṭha to the inhabitants of the earthly realm
questions.correct_answer: From Brahmā to the meanest insect
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Govinda maintains the manifested potencies of all jīvas, specifically mentioning the range from Brahmā to the meanest insect.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific categories of existence have their manifested potencies maintained by the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: Only the devas, the Vedas, and the righteous souls
All virtues, all vices, the Vedas, the penances, and all jīvas
The planets, the elements, and the material modes of nature
The spiritual sky, the causal ocean, and the material creation
questions.correct_answer: All virtues, all vices, the Vedas, the penances, and all jīvas
questions.explanation: The text lists virtues, vices, the Vedas, penances, and all jīvas as the potencies maintained by the power of Lord Govinda.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, whose conferred power is responsible for the maintenance of the manifested potencies found in all living beings and cosmic virtues?
options: Lord Brahmā
Lord Nārāyaṇa
Lord Govinda
Lord Śiva
questions.correct_answer: Lord Govinda
questions.explanation: The devotee-author explicitly adores the primeval Lord Govinda, noting that it is through His conferred power that these potencies are maintained.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, the maintenance of all virtues, vices, and penances is evidence of what aspect of the primeval Lord Govinda?
options: His ability to create the material atmosphere
His status as the source of all conferred power
His role in the administration of the planetary systems
His destruction of the material ego
questions.correct_answer: His status as the source of all conferred power
questions.explanation: The verse highlights that the existence of these potencies is due to the power conferred by Govinda, establishing Him as the supreme source.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which of the following is included among the potencies maintained by Lord Govinda?
options: The illusion of material beauty
The performance of penances
The arrogance of the demons
The rotation of the material spheres
questions.correct_answer: The performance of penances
questions.explanation: The verse specifically identifies 'penances' among the various potencies that exist by the power conferred by Lord Govinda.

54.verse_text: I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, who burns up to their roots all fruitive activities of those who are imbued with devotion and impartially ordains for each the due enjoyment of the fruits of one's activities, of all those who walk in the path of work, in accordance with the chain of their previously performed works, no less in the case of the tiny insect that bears the name of indragopa than in that of Indra, king of the devas.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what does the primeval Lord Govinda do to the fruitive activities of those who are truly imbued with devotion?
options: He rewards them with material opulence
He burns them up to their very roots
He transforms them into meditative practices
He ignores them in favor of silent prayer
questions.correct_answer: He burns them up to their very roots
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that for those imbued with devotion, Govinda burns up their fruitive activities to the roots.

questions.question: In Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, how does Govinda ordain the enjoyment of the fruits of activities for those walking in the path of work?
options: He grants all desires regardless of past deeds
He delegates the task to the demigods of the celestial planets
He impartially ordains them in accordance with their previously performed works
He intervenes to bypass the laws of causality for his devotees
questions.correct_answer: He impartially ordains them in accordance with their previously performed works
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Govinda impartially arranges the enjoyment of fruits based on the chain of one's previously performed works.

questions.question: Based on the text of Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, which two entities are cited to illustrate the impartiality of Govinda's arrangement of fruitive results?
options: Indra and the tiny indragopa insect
Brahma and the lowliest of men
The great sages and the material demons
The King of the devas and the smallest ant
questions.correct_answer: Indra and the tiny indragopa insect
questions.explanation: The verse specifically mentions Indra, the king of the devas, and the tiny insect known as indragopa as recipients of His impartial judgment.

questions.question: As stated in Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what is the nature of the activities that Lord Govinda ordains for those walking the path of work?
options: They are governed by the whims of the material nature
They are restricted to those who perform sacrifices
They correspond to the chain of previously performed works
They are meant to lead the soul back to the path of devotion immediately
questions.correct_answer: They correspond to the chain of previously performed works
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the fruitive results for those on the path of work are dictated by the chain of their past actions.

questions.question: According to Śrī Brahma-saṁhitā, what specific role does Govinda play regarding the fruitive results of living beings?
options: He is the passive observer of material laws
He impartially ordains the due enjoyment of fruits
He selectively alters the results to favor the righteous
He removes the necessity of fruitive labor for all beings
questions.correct_answer: He impartially ordains the due enjoyment of fruits
